phrases.intent_en: Ask where the gate for flight [number] is
phrases.text_local: Dov'è il gate per il volo [flight number]?
phrases.pronunciation: doh-VEH eel GAYT pehr eel VOH-loh
phrases.cultural_note: Italy's main airports: Fiumicino (Rome), Malpensa (Milan), Capodichino (Naples), Marco Polo (Venice). Gates in Italy are announced 20-30 minutes before boarding — don't leave the duty-free area too early. Gate numbers are shown on the departures board.

likely_response.local: Il suo gate è il B24, zona sud. Prenda il treno interno fino al terminal B.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel SOO-oh GAH-teh eh eel bee-VEHN-tee-KWAH-troh, DZOH-nah sood. PREN-dah eel TREH-noh een-TEHR-noh FEE-noh ahl tehr-mee-NAHL bee
likely_response.translation_en: Your gate is B24, south zone. Take the internal train to terminal B.

responses.english: Your gate is B24, south zone. Take the internal train to terminal B.
responses.phonetic: eel SOO-oh gayt EH eel bee-vehn-tee-KWAHT-troh, TSOH-nah sood. PREHN-dah eel TREH-noh een-TEHR-noh FEE-noh ahl teh-ree-mee-NAHL bee
responses.local: Il suo gate è il B24, zona sud. Prenda il treno interno fino al terminal B.

responses.english: Gate changes often, check the board.
responses.phonetic: eel gayt KAHM-byah SPEHS-soh, kohn-TROHL-lee eel tah-behl-LOH-neh
responses.local: Il gate cambia spesso, controlli il tabellone.

phrases.cultural_note: In Italy, luggage reclaim halls are clearly signposted as 'Ritiro Bagagli' or 'Bagagli'. After passport control, follow the signs. If your bag doesn't appear on the belt ('nastro'), report it immediately to the airline counter — don't leave the baggage hall.

likely_response.local: Il ritiro bagagli è al piano terra, uscita a destra.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel ree-TEE-roh bah-GAH-lyee eh ahl PYAH-noh TEHR-rah, oo-SHEE-tah ah DEHS-trah
likely_response.translation_en: Luggage claim is on the ground floor, exit to the right.

responses.english: Luggage claim is on the ground floor, exit to the right.
responses.phonetic: eel ree-TEE-roh bah-GAH-lee eh ahl PYAH-noh TEHR-rah, oo-SHEE-tah ah DEH-strah
responses.local: Il ritiro bagagli è al piano terra, uscita a destra.

responses.english: Your flight arrives at terminal one, belts three and four.
responses.phonetic: eel SOO-oh VOH-loh ahr-REE-vah ahl teh-ree-mee-NAHL OO-noh, NAH-stree treh eh KWAHT-troh
responses.local: Il suo volo arriva al terminal uno, nastri tre e quattro.

phrases.intent_en: Check if flight is on time
phrases.cultural_note: Italian airports have English-language boards alongside Italian. 'In orario' = on time, 'in ritardo' = delayed, 'cancellato' = cancelled, 'imbarco in corso' = boarding now.

likely_response.local: Sì, il volo è in orario, partirà alle 14:30.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, eel VOH-loh eh een oh-RAH-ryoh, pahr-tee-RAH al-leh KWAH-tor-deh-CHEE eh MEHZ-zoh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, the flight is on time, departing at 14:30.

responses.english: Yes, the flight is on time, departing at 14:30.
responses.phonetic: see, eel VOH-loh eh een oh-RAH-ryoh, pahr-tee-RAH AHL-leh kway-tohr-deh-MEElah
responses.local: Sì, il volo è in orario, partirà alle 14:30.

responses.english: The flight is delayed forty-five minutes due to weather.
responses.phonetic: eel VOH-loh ah oon ree-TAHR-doh dee kwah-rahn-tah-CHEEN-kweh mee-NOO-tee pehr MEH-teh-oh
responses.local: Il volo ha un ritardo di quarantacinque minuti per meteo.

phrases.text_local: C'è un deposito bagagli in aeroporto?
phrases.pronunciation: cheh oon deh-POH-zee-toh bah-GAH-lee een ah-eh-roh-POHR-toh
phrases.cultural_note: Major Italian airports (Fiumicino, Malpensa) have luggage storage. In Fiumicino it's near Terminal 3 entrance. Prices vary: typically €6-10 per bag per day. Some airports have 4-hour and 24-hour rates.

likely_response.local: Sì, il deposito è al piano terra, vicino all'uscita.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, eel deh-POH-zee-toh eh ahl PYAH-noh TEHR-rah, vee-CEE-noh ah-loo-SHEE-tah
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, luggage storage is on the ground floor, near the exit.

responses.english: Yes, luggage storage is on the ground floor, near the exit.
responses.phonetic: see, eel deh-POH-zee-toh eh ahl PYAH-noh TEHR-rah, vee-CHEE-noh ah-lah oo-SHEE-tah
responses.local: Sì, il deposito è al piano terra, vicino all'uscita.

responses.english: It costs eight euros per suitcase for six hours. More than six hours, twelve euros.
responses.phonetic: KOH-stah OHT-toh EH-oo-roh pehr vah-LEE-jah pehr seh-ee OH-reh. PYOO dee seh-ee OH-reh, DOH-dee-chee EH-oo-roh
responses.local: Costa otto euro per valigia per sei ore. Più di sei ore, dodici euro.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to buy city bus ticket
phrases.text_local: Dove posso comprare un biglietto dell'autobus per il centro?
phrases.pronunciation: DOH-veh POH-soh kohm-PRAH-reh oon bee-LYEHT-toh dehl-low-toh-BOOS pehr eel CHEHN-troh
phrases.cultural_note: Airport bus tickets in Italy: Rome Fiumicino has 'Sit Bus' and 'Terravision' to Termini. Milan Malpensa has 'Malpensa Express' train and 'Air Bus'. Tickets are usually bought at the bus ticket counter outside arrivals, or online in advance (cheaper).

likely_response.local: Il banco dei biglietti è all'uscita, a sinistra. L'autobus parte ogni trenta minuti.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel BAHN-koh day bee-LYEH-tee eh ahl-loo-SHEE-tah, ah see-NEE-strah. LAH-oo-toh-boos PAHR-teh OH-geh TREHN-tah MEE-noo-tee
likely_response.translation_en: The ticket desk is at the exit, on the left. The bus leaves every thirty minutes.

responses.english: The ticket desk is at the exit, on the left. The bus leaves every thirty minutes.
responses.phonetic: eel BAHN-koh day-ee bee-LYEHT-tee eh ah-loo-SHEE-tah, ah see-NEE-strah. low-TOH-boos PAHR-teh OHG-nee TREHN-tah mee-NOO-tee
responses.local: Il banco dei biglietti è all'uscita, a sinistra. L'autobus parte ogni trenta minuti.

responses.english: I recommend buying online, it's half the price.
responses.phonetic: kohn-SEE-lyoh dee kohm-PRAH-reh oh-NEE-lineh, KOH-stah lah meh-TAH
responses.local: Consiglio di comprare online, costa la metà.

phrases.text_local: Ho perso qualcosa a bordo dell'aereo.
phrases.pronunciation: oh PEHR-soh kwahl-KOH-zah ah BOHR-doh DEHL-lah-EH-reh-oh
phrases.cultural_note: If you left something on the plane, report it immediately at the airline counter before leaving the terminal. Items left in the cabin are logged and sometimes recovered. Items in checked luggage: report to the airline's baggage counter.

likely_response.local: Vada subito al banco della sua compagnia aerea, ritorniamo spesso oggetti dimenticati a bordo.
likely_response.pronunciation: VAH-dah SOO-bee-toh ahl BAHN-koh DEL-lah SOO-ah kohm-pah-NYEE-ah ah-EH-reh, ree-tor-NYAH-moh SPEH-soh ohg-JEHT-tee dee-mehn-TEE-kah-tee ah BOHR-doh
likely_response.translation_en: Go immediately to your airline counter, we often recover items left on board.

responses.english: Go immediately to your airline counter, we often recover items left on board.
responses.phonetic: VAH-dah soo-BEE-toh ahl BAHN-koh DEHL-lah soo-AH kohm-PAHN-yah ah-EH-reh-ah, ree-tohr-NYAH-moh spessoh oh-JEHT-tee dee-mehn-tee-KAH-tee ah BOHR-doh
responses.local: Vada subito al banco della sua compagnia aerea, ritorniamo spesso oggetti dimenticati a bordo.

responses.english: Fill out this form with a description of the item and your details.
responses.phonetic: KOHM-pee-lee KWES-toh MOH-doo-loh kohn lah deh-skree-TSYOH-neh dehl-loh-JEHT-toh eh ee SOO-eeee DAH-tee
responses.local: Compili questo modulo con la descrizione dell'oggetto e i suoi dati.

phrases.cultural_note: Italian bakeries ('panifici') sell fresh bread daily. The standard loaf is 'pane di按住' (rustic country bread). In Turin, 'grissini' (breadsticks) are the local specialty. Bread is sold by weight or by the loaf. Most panifici close on Sundays.

likely_response.local: Vuole il pane fresco di stamattina? L'ultimo è di ieri.
likely_response.pronunciation: vwoh-LEH eel PAH-neh FREHS-koh dee stah-maht-TEE-nah? LAHL-tee-moh eh dee YEH-ree
likely_response.translation_en: Do you want today's fresh bread from this morning? The last batch was from yesterday.

responses.english: Do you want today's fresh bread from this morning? The last batch was from yesterday.
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh eel PAH-neh FREHS-koh dee stah-maht-TEE-nah, LOOT-tee-moh eh dee YEH-ree
responses.local: Vuole il pane fresco di stamattina? L'ultimo è di ieri.

responses.english: Do you want it sliced or whole?
responses.phonetic: lo WOH-leh ah FAHT-teh oh een-TEH-roh
responses.local: Lo vuole a fette o intero?

phrases.cultural_note: Grissini were invented in Turin in the 17th century. They are sold by the bundle ('confezione'). Plain grissini are the classic, but 'grissini al sesamo' (sesame) and 'grissini al rosmarino' (rosemary) are also common.

likely_response.local: Quanti ne vuole? Vendiamo confezioni da sei o da dodici.
likely_response.pronunciation: KWAHN-tee neh VWOH-leh? vehn-DYAH-moh kon-feh-tsyoh-NEE dah SEH-ee oh dah DOH-dee-chee
likely_response.translation_en: How many do you want? We sell bundles of six or twelve.

responses.english: How many do you want? We sell bundles of six or twelve.
responses.phonetic: KWAHN-tee neh WOH-leh, vehn-DEE-ah-moh kohn-feh-TSYOH-nee dah SEH-ee oh dah DOH-dee-chee
responses.local: Quanti ne vuole? Vendiamo confezioni da sei o da dodici.

responses.english: Do you want plain or sesame?
responses.phonetic: lee WOH-leh sehm-PLEE-chee oh ahl seh-ZAH-moh
responses.local: Li vuole semplici o al sesamo?

phrases.cultural_note: Focaccia varies by region: Genoese focaccia is thin, oily, and often topped with olive oil and salt. Roman pizza bianca is thicker and often used for sandwiches. In Naples, 'focaccia di按住' is different again. Focaccia is often sold by weight ('etto' = 100g).

likely_response.local: Sì, l'abbiamo appena sfornata. Vuole assaggiare?
likely_response.pronunciation: see, LAHB-byah-moh ahp-PEH-nah sfor-NAH-tah. vwoh-LEH ahs-sah-JAH-reh?
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, we just took it out of the oven. Would you like to taste?

responses.english: Yes, we just took it out of the oven. Would you like to taste?
responses.phonetic: see, lahb-BYAH-moh ahp-PEH-nah sfohr-NAH-tah. WOH-leh ahs-sahj-JAH-reh
responses.local: Sì, l'abbiamo appena sfornata. Vuole assaggiare?

responses.english: How much do you want? One hundred grams, two hundred grams?
responses.phonetic: KWAHN-toh neh WOH-leh, oon EHT-toh, DOO-eh EHT-tee
responses.local: Quanto ne vuole? Un etto, due etti?

phrases.intent_en: Order bread and focaccia for picnic
phrases.text_local: Vorrei del pane e un po' di focaccia per un picnic.
phrases.pronunciation: vohr-RAY dehl PAH-neh eh oon poh dee foh-KAH-chah pehr oon PEEN-eek
phrases.cultural_note: Combining bread from a panificio with cold cuts from a salumeria is the classic Italian picnic lunch. Don't be surprised if the bakery doesn't have sliced bread — Italians buy whole loaves and slice at home.

likely_response.local: Certo! Le consiglio anche del nostro pane ai cereali, ottimo per picnic.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh! leh kon-SEE-lyoh AHN-keh dehl NOH-stroh PAH-neh ah-ee cheh-REH-lee, OHT-tee-moh pehr peek-NEEK
likely_response.translation_en: Of course! I also recommend our multi-grain bread, great for picnics.

responses.english: Of course! I also recommend our multi-grain bread, great for picnics.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, leh kohn-see-LYOH AHN-keh dehl NOHS-trohs PAH-neh eye cheh-reh-AH-lee, OHT-tee-moh pehr PEEN-eek
responses.local: Certo! Le consiglio anche del nostro pane ai cereali, ottimo per picnic.

responses.english: Unfortunately the focaccia is sold out, can I suggest some pizza bianca instead?
responses.phonetic: pohr-trohp-POH lah foh-KAH-chah eh fee-NEE-tah, POH-soh proh-pohr-LEH DEHL-lah PEET-sah BYAHN-kah
responses.local: Purtroppo la focaccia è finita, posso proporle della pizza bianca?

phrases.intent_en: Ask if card payment is accepted
phrases.cultural_note: Many small Italian panifici are cash-only. This is especially true in smaller towns and rural areas. Always ask before ordering if you only have a card.

likely_response.local: Mi dispiace, lavoriamo solo con contanti.
likely_response.pronunciation: mee dees-PYAH-cheh, lah-voh-RYAH-moh SOH-loh kon KAHN-tahn-tee
likely_response.translation_en: I'm sorry, we only deal with cash.

responses.english: I'm sorry, we only deal with cash.
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, lah-voh-RAH-yoh-moh SOH-loh kohn kohn-TAHN-tee
responses.local: Mi dispiace, lavoriamo solo con contanti.

responses.english: Yes, we accept cards and also digital payments.
responses.phonetic: see, ah-cheht-TYAH-moh KAHR-teh eh AHN-keh pah-gah-MEHN-tee eh-leh-TROH-nee-chee
responses.local: Sì, accettiamo carte e anche pagamenti elettronici.

phrases.text_local: Un panino con prosciutto e formaggio, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: oon pah-NEE-noh kohn proh-SHOO-toh eh for-MAHJ-joh, pehr fah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: A fresh panino from an Italian forno or panificio is a lunchtime staple. Fillings vary by region — in Rome ask for 'prosciutto e mozzarella'; in Tuscany 'lardo di Colonnata'; in Milan 'bresaola e rucola'. Point at the deli counter and the staff will guide you.

responses.english: I'll prepare it for you right away.
responses.phonetic: LYEH-loh preh-PAH-roh SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Glielo preparo subito.

responses.english: Which bread would you prefer? Ciabatta or a roll?
responses.phonetic: keh PAH-neh preh-feh-REE-sheh? chah-BAHT-tah oh roh-ZEHT-tah
responses.local: Che pane preferisce? Ciabatta o rosetta?

phrases.cultural_note: Italian bakeries ('panifici' or 'forni') typically bake bread in the early morning and again midday. Pastries ('dolci') vary by the day. This question often leads to a delightful local recommendation you'd never have discovered on your own.

responses.english: The house bread just came out of the oven.
responses.phonetic: eel PAH-neh dee KAH-zah eh ahp-PEH-nah oo-SHEE-toh dahl FOR-noh
responses.local: Il pane di casa è appena uscito dal forno.

responses.english: The focaccia is very fresh this morning.
responses.phonetic: lah foh-KAH-chah eh freh-SHEES-see-mah stah-maht-TEE-nah
responses.local: La focaccia è freschissima stamattina.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there are gluten-free options
phrases.cultural_note: Italy has one of the highest rates of diagnosed coeliac disease in Europe, so gluten-free ('senza glutine') awareness is high. Many Italian supermarkets and pharmacies stock a good range of gluten-free products. Specialist gluten-free bakeries ('panifici senza glutine') exist in larger cities.

responses.english: Yes, we have gluten-free bread and pastries.
responses.phonetic: SEE, ahb-BYAH-moh PAH-neh eh DOHL-chee SEHN-tsah GLOO-tee-neh
responses.local: Sì, abbiamo pane e dolci senza glutine.

responses.english: I'm sorry, we're not a gluten-free bakery.
responses.phonetic: mee dees-PYAH-cheh, nohn SYAH-moh OO-nah pah-neht-teh-REE-ah SEHN-tsah GLOO-tee-neh
responses.local: Mi dispiace, non siamo una panetteria senza glutine.

phrases.cultural_note: Simply point at the item you want and hold up two fingers — this combination works perfectly in any Italian bakery. Staff are used to tourists navigating by pointing, and it's perfectly good etiquette.

responses.english: Two croissants, is that right?
responses.phonetic: DOO-eh kor-NEHT-tee, vah BEH-neh
responses.local: Due cornetti, va bene?

responses.english: Here you go, two euros twenty.
responses.phonetic: EH-koh, DOO-eh EH-oo-roh eh VEHN-tee
responses.local: Ecco, due euro e venti.

phrases.cultural_note: Aperitivo is the Italian pre-dinner ritual, typically 7-9pm. You buy a drink (Spritz, Campari soda, wine) and a free buffet of snacks is included. This is NOT like American 'happy hour' — the food is often substantial enough to be a light meal.

likely_response.local: Prego! Il buffet è da quella parte.
likely_response.pronunciation: PREH-goh! eel boof-FEH eh dah KWEL-lah PAHR-teh
likely_response.translation_en: Here you go! The buffet is over there.

responses.english: Here you go! The buffet is over there.
responses.phonetic: PREH-goh, eel boo-FEH eh dah KWELL-lah PAHR-teh
responses.local: Prego! Il buffet è da quella parte.

responses.english: Would you like something to drink? We have Spritz, Aperol, Campari.
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh kwahl-KOH-zah dah BEH-reh, ahb-BYAH-moh SPRITZ, ah-peh-ROHL, kahm-PAH-ree
responses.local: Vuole qualcosa da bere? Abbiamo Spritz, Aperol, Campari.

responses.english: The buffet is included with your drink.
responses.phonetic: eel boo-FEH eh een-KLOO-zoh kohl DREENK
responses.local: Il buffet è incluso col drink.

phrases.cultural_note: Spritz Veneziano is the iconic aperitivo drink: Prosecco, Aperol (or Select, or Campari), and a splash of soda. Light, refreshing, slightly bitter. In Venice and the Veneto region it's an institution. Variations: Spritz Aperol (sweet), Spritz Select (more bitter), Spritz Campari (most bitter).

likely_response.local: Con Aperol, Select o Campari?
likely_response.pronunciation: kon ah-peh-ROHL, seh-LEHKT oh kahm-PAH-ree?
likely_response.translation_en: With Aperol, Select, or Campari?

responses.english: With Aperol, Select, or Campari?
responses.phonetic: kohn ah-peh-ROHL, seh-LEKT oh kahm-PAH-ree
responses.local: Con Aperol, Select o Campari?

responses.english: Here you are, cheers!
responses.phonetic: EH-koh ah lay-EE, sah-LOO-teh
responses.local: Ecco a lei, salute!

phrases.cultural_note: The buffet is usually self-service and buffet-style. Items vary: chips, olives, bruschetta, mini sandwiches, pasta salads. Take as much as you want — but don't treat it as a dinner substitute.

likely_response.local: È là in fondo, si serva pure.
likely_response.pronunciation: eh LAH een FOHN-doh, see sehr-VAH POO-reh
likely_response.translation_en: It's over there in the back, help yourself.

responses.english: It's over there in the back, help yourself.
responses.phonetic: eh lah een FOHN-goh, see SEHR-vah POO-reh
responses.local: È là in fondo, si serva pure.

responses.english: The buffet is at the counter, self-service.
responses.phonetic: eel boo-FEH eh ahl bahn-KOH-neh self-SEHR-viis
responses.local: Il buffet è al bancone self-service.

phrases.intent_en: Order a glass of red wine
phrases.text_local: Un bicchiere di vino rosso, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: oon bee-KYEH-reh dee VEE-noh ROHS-soh, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Wine by the glass ('al bicchiere') is common at Italian bars during aperitivo. Ask for red ('rosso'), white ('bianco'), or rosé ('rosato'). Italian regions each have their own DOC wines — bartenders are usually happy to recommend.

likely_response.local: Ha preferenze di regione? Abbiamo vini dal Veneto, dalla Toscana, dalla Sicilia.
likely_response.pronunciation: ah preh-feh-REHN-tseh dee reh-JYOH-neh? AHB-byah-moh VEE-nee dahl VEH-neh-toh, DAHL-lah TOHS-kah-nah, DAHL-lah see-CHEE-lyah
likely_response.translation_en: Do you have a regional preference? We have wines from Veneto, Tuscany, Sicily.

responses.english: Do you have a regional preference? We have wines from Veneto, Tuscany, Sicily.
responses.phonetic: ah preh-feh-REHN-tsee dee reh-JOH-neh, ahb-BYAH-moh VEE-nee dahl veh-NEH-toh, DAHL-lah toh-SKAH-nah, DAHL-lah see-CHEE-lah
responses.local: Ha preferenze di regione? Abbiamo vini dal Veneto, dalla Toscana, dalla Sicilia.

responses.english: Do you want a 20cl or 10cl glass?
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh eel bee-KYEH-reh dah VEHN-tee oh dah DYEH-chee chih-LOH
responses.local: Vuole il bicchiere da venti o da dieci cl?

phrases.cultural_note: Unlike restaurants, bar tabs in Italy are usually paid at the register before leaving. In some bars the waiter brings the check to your table. Tipping is not expected — round up or leave small change.

likely_response.local: Vada pure alla cassa, grazie.
likely_response.pronunciation: VAH-dah POO-reh AHL-lah KAHS-sah, GRAH-tsyeh
likely_response.translation_en: Please go to the register, thank you.

responses.english: Please go to the register, thank you.
responses.phonetic: VAH-dah POO-reh AHL-lah KAHS-sah, GRAH-tsyeh
responses.local: Vada pure alla cassa, grazie.

responses.english: That's three euros and fifty cents.
responses.phonetic: SOH-noh treh EH-oo-roh eh cheen-KWAHN-tah
responses.local: Sono tre euro e cinquanta.

phrases.text_local: Un'acqua minerale, per favore. Naturale o frizzante?
phrases.pronunciation: oo-NAH-kwah mee-neh-RAH-leh, pehr pah-VOH-reh. nah-too-RAH-leh oh freet-TSAHN-teh
phrases.cultural_note: Italians debate still vs sparkling water ('naturale o frizzante') seriously. San Pellegrino is the famous sparkling brand. Both are commonly served. At bars, sparkling water costs more than still.

likely_response.local: Naturale o frizzante?
likely_response.pronunciation: nah-too-RAH-leh oh freet-SAHN-teh?
likely_response.translation_en: Still or sparkling?

responses.english: Still or sparkling?
responses.phonetic: nah-too-RAH-leh oh freet-TSAHN-teh
responses.local: Naturale o frizzante?

responses.english: Here you are, thank you.
responses.phonetic: EH-koh ah lay-EE, GRAH-tsyeh
responses.local: Ecco a lei, grazie.

phrases.intent_en: Order another round of the same
phrases.text_local: La stessa cosa per tutti, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: lah STEHS-sah KOH-zah pehr TOOT-tee, pehr fah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: In Italy individual ordering is more common than rounds, especially during aperitivo. But at informal bars and osterie this phrase works perfectly for a group.

likely_response.local: Subito! Le stesse cose per tutti.
likely_response.pronunciation: SOO-bee-toh! leh STEHS-seh KOH-seh pehr TOOT-tee
likely_response.translation_en: Right away! The same for everyone.

responses.english: Right away! The same for everyone.
responses.phonetic: SOO-bee-toh, leh STEHS-seh KOH-seh pehr TOOT-tee
responses.local: Subito! Le stesse cose per tutti.

responses.english: How many people?
responses.phonetic: KWAHN-teh pehr-SOH-neh
responses.local: Quante persone?

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the bar closes
phrases.cultural_note: Italian bars typically close around midnight to 1am. Aperitivo bars wind down by 10pm. Ask early to plan the rest of your evening.

likely_response.local: Chiudiamo all'una di notte.
likely_response.pronunciation: kyoo-DYAH-moh ahl-LOO-nah dee NOHT-teh
likely_response.translation_en: We close at 1am.

responses.english: We close at 1am.
responses.phonetic: kyoo-DYAH-moh ahl-LOO-nah dee NOHT-teh
responses.local: Chiudiamo all'una di notte.

responses.english: Tonight we close at midnight.
responses.phonetic: stah-SEH-rah kyoo-DYAH-moh ah meht-tsah-NOHT-teh
responses.local: Stasera chiudiamo a mezzanotte.

phrases.cultural_note: Common non-alcoholic options in Italian bars: acqua tonica, gassosa, aranciata (fizzy orange), chinotto (bitter citrus soda), San Bitter (non-alcoholic aperitif).

likely_response.local: Abbiamo succhi, acqua tonica, aranciata, chinotto…
likely_response.pronunciation: ahb-BYAH-moh SOOK-kee, AH-kwah TOH-nee-kah, ah-rahn-CHAH-tah, kee-NOHT-toh
likely_response.translation_en: We have juices, tonic water, fizzy orange, chinotto…

responses.english: We have juices, tonic water, fizzy orange, chinotto…
responses.phonetic: ahb-BYAH-moh SOOK-kee, AH-kwah TOH-nee-kah, ah-rahn-CHAH-tah, kee-NOHT-toh
responses.local: Abbiamo succhi, acqua tonica, aranciata, chinotto…

responses.english: Would you like a Crodino or San Bitter?
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh oon kroh-DEE-noh oh oon san BIT-tehr
responses.local: Vuole un Crodino o un San Bitter?

phrases.intent_en: Ask if they have bar snacks
phrases.cultural_note: At aperitivo bars, snacks are often included with your drink. At regular bars, look for tramezzini (triangle sandwiches), bruschette, or stuzzichini (nibbles) at the counter.

likely_response.local: Sì, il buffet è incluso con la consumazione.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, eel boo-FEH eh een-KLOO-zoh kohn lah kohn-soo-mah-TSYOH-neh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, the buffet is included with your drink.

responses.english: Yes, the buffet is included with your drink.
responses.phonetic: see, eel boo-FEH eh een-KLOO-zoh kohn lah kohn-soo-mah-TSYOH-neh
responses.local: Sì, il buffet è incluso con la consumazione.

responses.english: We have sandwiches and nibbles at the counter.
responses.phonetic: ahb-BYAH-moh trah-meht-TSEE-nee eh stoots-tsee-KEE-nee ahl bahn-KOH-neh
responses.local: Abbiamo tramezzini e stuzzichini al bancone.

phrases.cultural_note: Running a tab is less common at Italian bars — many prefer payment per drink at the counter. At sit-down venues with table service, the waiter tracks your order and you pay at the end.

likely_response.local: Sì, a che nome?
likely_response.pronunciation: see, ah keh NOH-meh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, what name?

responses.english: Yes, what name?
responses.phonetic: see, ah keh NOH-meh
responses.local: Sì, a che nome?

responses.english: We prefer immediate payment, thank you.
responses.phonetic: preh-feh-RYAH-moh eel pah-gah-MEHN-toh eem-meh-DYAH-toh, GRAH-tsyeh
responses.local: Preferiamo il pagamento immediato, grazie.

phrases.text_local: Una bottiglia di vino, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: OO-nah boht-TEE-lyah dee VEE-noh, pehr fah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Ordering a bottle ('una bottiglia') is great value at Italian bars and osterie. 'Vino della casa' (house wine) is typically local, well-chosen, and affordable.

likely_response.local: Rosso, bianco o rosato?
likely_response.pronunciation: ROHS-soh, BYAHN-koh oh roh-ZAH-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Red, white, or rosé?

responses.english: Red, white, or rosé?
responses.phonetic: ROHS-soh, BYAHN-koh oh roh-ZAH-toh
responses.local: Rosso, bianco o rosato?

responses.english: We have excellent local wines.
responses.phonetic: ahb-BYAH-moh OHT-tee-mee VEE-nee loh-KAH-lee
responses.local: Abbiamo ottimi vini locali.

phrases.intent_en: Ask price for umbrella and two sunbeds
phrases.text_local: Quanto costa un ombrellone e due sdraio?
phrases.pronunciation: KWAHN-toh KOH-stah oon ohm-brel-LOH-neh eh DOO-eh ZDRAH-yoh
phrases.cultural_note: Italian beaches (except public stretches) charge for sunbeds ('sdraio') and umbrellas ('ombrellone'). Prices vary wildly between resort beaches (€15-30/day in places like Forte dei Marmi or the Amalfi Coast) and smaller local beaches. Always confirm the price before settling in.

likely_response.local: L'ombrellone con due sdraio costa venti euro per la giornata.
likely_response.pronunciation: lohm-breh-LLOH-neh kon DWEH SDRAH-yoh KOH-stah VEHN-tee EH-oo-roh pehr lah johr-NAH-tah
likely_response.translation_en: The umbrella with two sunbeds costs twenty euros for the day.

responses.english: The umbrella with two sunbeds costs twenty euros for the day.
responses.phonetic: loom-brel-LOH-neh kohn DOO-eh ZDRAH-yoh KOH-stah VEHN-tee EH-oo-roh pehr lah jhoor-NAH-tah
responses.local: L'ombrellone con due sdraio costa venti euro per la giornata.

responses.english: Do you have a position preference? Front row or back row?
responses.phonetic: ah preh-feh-REHN-tsah dee POHS-toh, PREE-mah FEE-lah oh seh-KOHN-dah FEE-lah
responses.local: Ha preferenza di posto? Prima fila o seconda fila?

phrases.intent_en: Ask where nearest free beach is
phrases.text_local: C'è una spiaggia libera qui vicino?
phrases.pronunciation: cheh OO-nah SPYAH-jah LEE-beh-rah koo-ee vee-CHEE-noh
phrases.cultural_note: 'Spiaggia libera' = public beach (free). Many Italian beaches have a mix of private beach clubs ('stabilimenti') and free public areas. In peak season, public beaches can be very crowded. Look for signs: 'spiaggia libera' or 'libero'. In Venice Lido, there are both free and paid sections.

likely_response.local: Sì, la spiaggia libera è a trecento metri verso nord, dopo il stabilimento.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, lah spyah-JAH LEE-beh-rah eh ah treh-CHEN-toh MEH-tree VEHR-soh nohrd, AH-peh-soh eel stah-bee-lee-MEHN-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, the public beach is three hundred meters north, after the beach club.

responses.english: Yes, the public beach is three hundred meters north, after the beach club.
responses.phonetic: see, lah SPYAH-jah LEE-beh-rah eh ah treh-CHEHN-toh MEH-tree VEHR-soh nohrd, DOH-pah eel stah-bee-lee-MEHN-toh
responses.local: Sì, la spiaggia libera è a trecento metri verso nord, dopo il stabilimento.

responses.english: Unfortunately this area is all private beach clubs. The nearest public beach is one kilometer away.
responses.phonetic: poort-TROHP-poh koo-ee een-TOHR-noh EH TOOT-toh stah-bee-lee-MEHN-tee pree-VAH-tee. lah SPYAH-jah LEE-beh-rah ploo vee-CHEE-nah eh ah oon kee-LOH-meh-troh
responses.local: Purtroppo qui intorno è tutto stabilimenti privati. La spiaggia libera più vicina è a un chilometro.

phrases.intent_en: Ask price for lunch at beach club
phrases.text_local: Quanto costa un pranzo qui allo stabilimento?
phrases.pronunciation: KWAHN-toh KOH-stah oon PRAHN-tsoh koo-ee AHL-loh stah-bee-lee-MEHN-toh
phrases.cultural_note: Beach club restaurants ('ristorante dello stabilimento') in Italy are often surprisingly good — fresh fish, local pasta. Prices at beach clubs on the Amalfi Coast or Capri can be very high. Always ask for the menu ('la carta') before ordering.

likely_response.local: Il pranzo completo con primo, secondo e bibite parte da trentacinque euro a persona.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel prah-NOH kohm-PLEH-toh kon PREH-moh, seh-KOHN-doh eh BEE-bee-teh PAHR-teh dah trehn-tah-CHEEN-kweh EH-oo-roh ah pehr-SOH-nah
likely_response.translation_en: Full lunch with first course, main course, and drinks starts from thirty-five euros per person.

responses.english: Full lunch with first course, main course, and drinks starts from thirty-five euros per person.
responses.phonetic: eel PRAHN-tsoh kohm-PLOH-toh kohn PREE-moh, seh-KOHN-doh eh bee-BEE-teh PAHR-teh dah trehn-tah-CHEEN-kwah EH-oo-roh ah pehr-SOH-nah
responses.local: Il pranzo completo con primo, secondo e bibite parte da trentacinque euro a persona.

responses.english: We also have a lunch menu at twenty euros, first course and a drink.
responses.phonetic: ahb-BYAH-moh AHN-keh oon meh-NOO PRAHN-tsoh ah VEHN-tee EH-oo-roh, PREE-moh eh bee-BEE-tah
responses.local: Abbiamo anche un menu pranzo a venti euro, primo e bibita.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the water is safe for swimming
phrases.cultural_note: Italian beaches are generally clean and safe for swimming. However, in some areas (especially near river mouths or harbors), water quality can be poor — look for 'bandiera blu' (Blue Flag) certification which indicates clean water. At Venice Lido, water quality varies by section.

likely_response.local: Sì, l'acqua è ottima, abbiamo la bandiera blu.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, LAH-kwah eh OHT-tee-mah, AHB-byah-moh lah BAHN-dee-rah BLOO
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, the water is excellent, we have the Blue Flag.

responses.english: Yes, the water is excellent, we have the Blue Flag.
responses.phonetic: see,LAH-kwah eh OHT-tee-mah, ahb-BYAH-moh lah bahn-DYEH-rah bloo
responses.local: Sì, l'acqua è ottima, abbiamo la bandiera blu.

responses.english: Be careful, today there's a strong current, swim close to the shore.
responses.phonetic: aht-tehn-TSYOH-neh, OH-jee cheh KOHR-rehn-teh FOHR-teh, NWOH-tee VEE-chyoh AHL-lah REE-vah
responses.local: Attenzione, oggi c'è corrente forte, nuoti vicino alla riva.

phrases.intent_en: Order a water and a coffee
phrases.text_local: Un'acqua e un caffè, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: oo-NAH-kwah eh oon kah-FEH, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Beach bars ('chiosco' or 'bar dello stabilimento') serve drinks and light food. Coffee at the beach is common in Italy — a 'caffè shakerato' (shaken iced espresso) is a summer classic. Beach bar prices are always higher than city bars.

likely_response.local: Ecco a lei. Sono cinque euro, grazie.
likely_response.pronunciation: EHK-koh ah LEH-ee. SOH-noh CHEEN-kweh EH-oo-roh, GRAH-tsyeh
likely_response.translation_en: Here you are. That's five euros, thank you.

responses.english: Here you are. That's five euros, thank you.
responses.phonetic: EH-koh ah lay-EE, SOH-noh CHEEN-kweh EH-oo-roh, GRAH-tsyeh
responses.local: Ecco a lei. Sono cinque euro, grazie.

responses.english: Would you also like a gelato? Today we have fresh pistachio.
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh AHN-keh oon jeh-LAH-toh, OH-jee ahb-BYAH-moh eel pees-TAHK-kyoh FREHS-koh
responses.local: Vuole anche un gelato? Oggi abbiamo il pistacchio fresco.

phrases.cultural_note: In Italy, 'un caffè' always means an espresso — NOT a large coffee. You stand at the bar, pay first at the cash register ('cassa'), then show the receipt to the barista. This is cheaper than sitting at a table.

likely_response.local: Prego!
likely_response.pronunciation: PREH-goh
likely_response.translation_en: Here you go!

responses.english: Here you go!
responses.phonetic: PREH-goh
responses.local: Prego!

responses.english: Do you want it sweetened?
responses.phonetic: loh WOH-leh tsook-keh-RAH-toh
responses.local: Lo vuole zuccherato?

responses.english: Coming right up! Here you are.
responses.phonetic: see-SEE-see-moh EH-koh ah lay-EE
responses.local: Sissimo! Ecco a lei.

phrases.cultural_note: Cappuccino is a breakfast drink in Italy — ordering one after 11am marks you as a tourist. If you must have it in the afternoon, say 'cappuccino macchiato' (espresso with a drop of milk). Never ask for 'latte macchiato' after morning.

likely_response.local: Prego! Grand o o piccolo?
likely_response.pronunciation: PREH-goh! grahn-doh oh pee-KOH-loh?
likely_response.translation_en: Here you go! Large or small?

responses.english: Here you go! Large or small?
responses.phonetic: PREH-goh, grahnd oh oh PEEK-koh-loh
responses.local: Prego! Grand o o piccolo?

responses.english: I'll bring it right away.
responses.phonetic: loh POHR-toh soo-BEE-toh
responses.local: Lo porto subito.

responses.english: With or without foam?
responses.phonetic: kohn oh SEHN-tsah SKYOO-mah
responses.local: Con o senza schiuma?

phrases.intent_en: Tell staff you paid at the till
phrases.cultural_note: This is critical: in Italian bars, you MUST pay at the register first. If you sit without paying at cassa first, you'll be embarrassed when the waiter tells you to pay before ordering. Show your receipt ('scontrino') to the barista.

likely_response.local: Sì, mi faccia vedere lo scontrino.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, mee FAH-chah veh-DEH-reh loh skohn-TREE-noh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, let me see the receipt.

responses.english: Yes, let me see the receipt.
responses.phonetic: see, mee FAH-chah veh-DEH-reh lo skohn-TREE-noh
responses.local: Sì, mi faccia vedere lo scontrino.

responses.english: Thanks, go ahead to the counter.
responses.phonetic: GRAH-tsyeh, VAH-dah POO-reh ahl bahn-KOH-neh
responses.local: Grazie, vada pure al bancone.

responses.english: Already paid? Great, coming right up.
responses.phonetic: ah yah PAH-gah-toh, BEH-neh, ah-RREE-voh
responses.local: Ha già pagato? Bene, arrivo.

phrases.cultural_note: Cornetti are the standard Italian breakfast pastry — similar to a croissant but often less buttery. They come plain or filled with jam, Nutella, or custard. Sold at the bar for a low price — eat standing with your coffee.

likely_response.local: Vuole il cornetto alla crema o alla marmellata?
likely_response.pronunciation: vwoh-LEH eel kor-NET-toh AHL-lah KREH-mah oh AHL-lah mar-meh-LAH-tah?
likely_response.translation_en: Would you like it with cream or jam?

responses.english: Would you like it with cream or jam?
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh eel kohr-NEHT-toh AHL-lah KREH-mah oh AHL-lah mahr-meh-LAH-tah
responses.local: Vuole il cornetto alla crema o alla marmellata?

responses.english: Here you are, enjoy your breakfast!
responses.phonetic: EH-koh ah lay-EE, BWOHS-nah koh-loh-TSYOH-neh
responses.local: Ecco a lei, buona colazione!

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there’s an outdoor table
phrases.cultural_note: Outdoor seating ('tavolo fuori' or 'tavolo all'aperto') typically costs significantly more than standing at the bar. This is normal — the extra charge covers the server's time. Don't be surprised if a coffee costs €3-5 outside vs €1 at the bar.

likely_response.local: Sì, si accomodi pure!
likely_response.pronunciation: see, see ahk-KOH-moh-dee POO-reh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, please, take a seat!

responses.english: Yes, please, take a seat!
responses.phonetic: see, see ahk-koh-MOH-dee POO-reh
responses.local: Sì, si accomodi pure!

responses.english: I'm sorry, it's all occupied.
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, eh TOOT-toh ok-koo-PAH-toh
responses.local: Mi dispiace, è tutto occupato.

responses.english: The outdoor table costs an extra two euros.
responses.phonetic: eel TAH-voh-loh FWOH-ree KOH-stah oon soop-pleh-MEHN-toh dee DOO-eh EH-oo-roh
responses.local: Il tavolo fuori costa un supplemento di due euro.

phrases.cultural_note: Espresso macchiato = espresso with a small drop of milk (the 'macchia' = stain). Different from 'caffè latte' which is mostly milk. Also different from 'macchiato caldo' which is a larger milk coffee. The macchiato is the classic mid-morning Italian coffee.

likely_response.local: Subito!
likely_response.pronunciation: SOO-bee-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Right away!

responses.english: Right away!
responses.phonetic: SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Subito!

responses.english: With a little or a lot of milk?
responses.phonetic: kohn POH-koh oh TAHN-toh LAHT-teh
responses.local: Con poco o tanto latte?

phrases.cultural_note: Most Italian bars and cafés have WiFi, but the culture is to linger over coffee rather than work. Ask at the counter and you'll usually get a small card with the code, or be pointed to a sign.

responses.english: Yes, the password is on the sign.
responses.phonetic: SEE, lah PAHSS-word eh sool kar-TEHL-loh
responses.local: Sì, la password è sul cartello.

responses.english: It's written on the till.
responses.phonetic: eh SKREE-tah SOO-lah KAHS-sah
responses.local: È scritta sulla cassa.

phrases.cultural_note: Italian bar culture is built around standing at the counter for a quick espresso. Takeaway ('da asporto') is growing in cities but is still less common than in northern Europe — some traditional bars may not offer it.

responses.english: Of course, in a paper cup.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, een oon beek-KYEH-reh dee KAR-tah
responses.local: Certo, in un bicchiere di carta.

responses.english: I'm sorry, we don't do takeaway coffee.
responses.phonetic: mee dees-PYAH-cheh, nohn ahb-BYAH-moh
responses.local: Mi dispiace, non abbiamo il caffè da asporto.

phrases.cultural_note: Sugar sachets are usually already on the counter in an Italian bar. Italians often stir sugar into their espresso before drinking. If you don't see any, just ask the barista.

responses.english: Yes, it's on the counter.
responses.phonetic: SEE, eh sool bahn-KOH-neh
responses.local: Sì, è sul bancone.

responses.english: Here's the sugar.
responses.phonetic: EH-koh loh TSOOK-keh-roh
responses.local: Ecco lo zucchero.

phrases.cultural_note: A wonderful phrase in any Italian bar or pasticceria. The barista or staff will often suggest whatever is freshest or the house speciality — trust them. Regional pastries like maritozzi (Rome) or sfogliatelle (Naples) are worth discovering.

responses.english: I recommend the maritozzo with cream.
responses.phonetic: leh kohn-SEEL-yoh eel mah-ree-TOT-tsoh kohn lah PAHN-nah
responses.local: Le consiglio il maritozzo con la panna.

responses.english: The sfogliatella is excellent today.
responses.phonetic: lah sfohl-yah-TEHL-lah eh bwoh-NEES-see-mah OH-jee
responses.local: La sfogliatella è buonissima oggi.

phrases.intent_en: Order a glass of red wine
phrases.text_local: Un bicchiere di vino rosso, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: oon bee-KYEH-reh dee VEE-noh ROHS-soh, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Wine by the glass ('al bicchiere') is standard at Italian enoteche. The price varies by region and quality — house wine ('vino della casa') is always safe. Italians pair wine by region: Chianti with Tuscan food, Barolo with Piedmont dishes, Nero d'Avola with Sicilian.

likely_response.local: Ha una regione preferita? Abbiamo vini dal Veneto, dalla Toscana, dalla Sicilia.
likely_response.pronunciation: ah OO-nah reh-JOH-neh preh-feh-REE-tah? AH-byah-moh VEE-nee dahl VEH-neh-toh, DAHL-lah TOS-kah-nah, DAHL-lah see-CHEE-lyah
likely_response.translation_en: Do you have a preferred region? We have wines from Veneto, Tuscany, Sicily.

responses.english: Do you have a preferred region? We have wines from Veneto, Tuscany, Sicily.
responses.phonetic: ah OO-nah reh-JOH-neh preh-feh-REE-tah, ahb-BYAH-moh VEE-nee dahl veh-NEH-toh, DAHL-lah toh-SKAH-nah, DAHL-lah see-CHEE-lah
responses.local: Ha una regione preferita? Abbiamo vini dal Veneto, dalla Toscana, dalla Sicilia.

responses.english: The house red is a Chianti Classico, excellent.
responses.phonetic: eel ROHS-soh DEHL-lah KAH-zah eh oon KYAHN-tee KLah-see-koh, OHT-tee-moh
responses.local: Il rosso della casa è un Chianti classico, ottimo.

phrases.cultural_note: Italian enoteca staff are often deeply knowledgeable about wine. Tell them what you're eating or what you like and they'll find something appropriate. Don't be intimidated — they're usually enthusiastic about helping.

likely_response.local: Se mangia pesce, le consiglio un Vermentino sardo. Se preferisce carne, un Negroamaro dal Salento.
likely_response.pronunciation: seh MAHN-jah PEH-sheh, leh kon-SEE-lyoh oon vehr-men-TEE-noh SAR-doh. Seh preh-FEH-ree-sheh KAR-neh, oon neh-groh-ah-MAH-roh dahl sah-LEN-toh
likely_response.translation_en: If you're eating fish, I recommend a Sardinian Vermentino. If you prefer meat, a Negroamaro from Salento.

responses.english: If you're eating fish, I recommend a Sardinian Vermentino. If you prefer meat, a Negroamaro from Salento.
responses.phonetic: seh mahn-JEE-ah PEHS-keh, leh kohn-see-LYOH oon vehr-mehn-TEE-noh SAHR-doh. seh preh-feh-REE-seh KAHR-neh, oon neh-goh-ah-MAH-roh dahl sah-LEHN-toh
responses.local: Se mangia pesce, le consiglio un Vermentino sardo. Se preferisce carne, un Negroamaro dal Salento.

responses.english: This red is a Brunello di Montalcino, aged five years.
responses.phonetic: KWES-toh ROHS-soh eh oon broo-NEHL-loh dee mohn-tahl-CHEE-noh, een-vehk-KYAH-toh CHEEN-kweh AH-nee
responses.local: Questo rosso è un Brunello di Montalcino, invecchiato cinque anni.

phrases.intent_en: Ask price of a glass of wine
phrases.cultural_note: Wine prices at enoteche can vary wildly — a glass of house wine is €4-6, while a premium Barolo can be €15-25 per bicchiere. Always ask the price first if not displayed.

likely_response.local: Il nostro Negroamaro è otto euro al bicchiere.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel NOH-stroh neh-groh-ah-MAH-roh eh OH-toh EH-oo-roh ahl bee-KYEH-reh
likely_response.translation_en: Our Negroamaro is eight euros per glass.

responses.english: Our Negroamaro is eight euros per glass.
responses.phonetic: eel NOHS-trohs neh-goh-ah-MAH-roh EH OHT-toh EH-oo-roh ahl bee-KYEH-reh
responses.local: Il nostro Negroamaro è otto euro al bicchiere.

responses.english: It depends on the glass — 10cl or 20cl?
responses.phonetic: dee-PEHN-deh dahl bee-KYEH-reh, DYEH-chee cl oh VEHN-tee cl
responses.local: Dipende dal bicchiere — dieci cl o venti cl?

phrases.intent_en: Order a charcuterie and cheese board
phrases.text_local: Un tagliere di salumi e formaggi, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: oon tah-LYEH-reh dee sah-LOO-mee eh fohr-MAH-jee, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: A 'tagliere' is a mixed platter of cold cuts and cheeses, standard at enoteche. Typically includes prosciutto di Parma, salame, pecorino, and parmesan. Perfect for sharing and pairing with wine.

likely_response.local: Che formaggio preferisce? Abbiamo pecorino, parmigiano, mozzarella di bufala.
likely_response.pronunciation: keh for-MAHJ-joh preh-FEH-ree-sheh? AH-byah-moh peh-koh-REE-noh, par-mee-JAH-noh, mot-tsah-REHL-lah dee BOO-fah-lah
likely_response.translation_en: Which cheese do you prefer? We have pecorino, parmesan, buffalo mozzarella.

responses.english: Which cheese do you prefer? We have pecorino, parmesan, buffalo mozzarella.
responses.phonetic: keh fohr-MAH-joh preh-feh-REE-sheh, ahb-BYAH-moh peh-choh-REE-noh, pahr-mee-JAH-noh, moh-tzah-REHL-lah dee boo-FAH-lah
responses.local: Che formaggio preferisce? Abbiamo pecorino, parmigiano, mozzarella di bufala.

responses.english: How large do you want it?
responses.phonetic: KWAHN-toh lo WOH-leh GRAHN-deh
responses.local: Quanto lo vuole grande?

phrases.intent_en: Order a glass of wine and pay at till
phrases.text_local: Un bicchiere di..., per favore. Pago alla cassa.
phrases.pronunciation: oon bee-KYEH-reh dee..., pehr pah-VOH-reh. PAH-goh AHL-lah KAHS-sah
phrases.cultural_note: At many Italian enoteche, standing at the bar with your wine is perfectly acceptable and often the local way. You pay at the register, show your receipt, receive your wine. Sitting at a table usually costs more.

likely_response.local: Ecco a lei. Buon appetito!
likely_response.pronunciation: EK-koh ah LEH-ee. bwohn ahp-peh-TEE-toh!
likely_response.translation_en: Here you are. Enjoy!

responses.english: Here you are. Enjoy!
responses.phonetic: EH-koh ah lay-EE, bwohn ahp-peh-TEE-toh
responses.local: Ecco a lei. Buon appetito!

responses.english: Pay at the register? Perfect, go right ahead.
responses.phonetic: PAH-goh AHL-lah KAHS-sah, pehr-FEHT-toh, VAH-dah POO-reh
responses.local: Pago alla cassa? Perfetto, vada pure.

phrases.intent_en: Order a bottle of wine to take away
phrases.text_local: Vorrei comprare una bottiglia di... da portare via.
phrases.pronunciation: vohr-RAY kohm-PRAH-reh OO-nah boht-TEE-lyah dee... dah pohr-TAH-reh VEE-ah
phrases.cultural_note: Enoteche often sell bottles at retail prices. If you find a wine you love, ask if you can buy a bottle — most are happy to sell. Tax refund is available for purchases over €154.94 at participating stores.

likely_response.local: Certo! Le preparo la bottiglia. Ha bisogno di un imballo per il viaggio?
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh! leh preh-PAH-roh lah bot-TEE-lyah. ah bee-ZOH-nyoh deh oon eem-BAHL-loh pehr eel vee-AHJ-joh?
likely_response.translation_en: Of course! I'll prepare the bottle for you. Do you need packaging for the trip?

responses.english: Of course! I'll prepare the bottle for you. Do you need packaging for the trip?
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, leh preh-PRAH-oh lah boht-TEE-lyah. ah bee-ZOHG-nyoh dee oon eem-BAH-loh pehr eel VYAH-joh
responses.local: Certo! Le preparo la bottiglia. Ha bisogno di un imballo per il viaggio?

responses.english: For purchases over 150 euros you can request a tax refund.
responses.phonetic: pehr ah-KWEE-stee SOH-prah chehn-toh-cheen-KWAHN-tah EH-oo-roh pwoh ree-KYEH-deh-reh eel reem-BOHR-soh fee-SKAH-leh
responses.local: Per acquisti sopra centocinquanta euro può richiedere il rimborso fiscale.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to taste a gelato flavour
phrases.cultural_note: 'Posso assaggiare?' is always acceptable and expected — never feel shy about tasting before buying. Gelaterias expect this. Flavors change seasonally — what's available today may not be there tomorrow.

likely_response.local: Certo! Quale vuole assaggiare?
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh! KWAH-leh vwoh-LEH as-sah-JAH-reh?
likely_response.translation_en: Of course! Which one would you like to taste?

responses.english: Of course! Which one would you like to taste?
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, KWAH-leh WOH-leh ahs-sahj-JAH-reh
responses.local: Certo! Quale vuole assaggiare?

responses.english: This is pistachio, one of our best.
responses.phonetic: KWES-toh eh pees-TAHK-kyoh, OO-noh day NOHS-treee mee-LYOH-ree
responses.local: Questo è pistacchio, uno dei nostri migliori.

phrases.intent_en: Order a medium cup of gelato
phrases.text_local: Una coppetta media con..., per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: OO-nah kohp-PEHT-tah MEH-dyah kohn..., pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Order by saying how many flavors ('gusti') and the size. Small = 1-2 flavors, Medium = 2-3 flavors, Large = 3+ flavors. 'Coppetta' = cup, 'cono' = cone. Don't confuse artisan gelato with industrial brands (Grom, Venchi) — artisan is usually labelled 'gelateria artigianale'.

likely_response.local: Quanti gusti desidera?
likely_response.pronunciation: KWAHN-tee GOOS-tee deh-zee-DEH-rah?
likely_response.translation_en: How many flavors would you like?

responses.english: How many flavors would you like?
responses.phonetic: KWAHN-tee GOOS-tee deh-see-DEH-rah
responses.local: Quanti gusti desidera?

responses.english: Do you want a cone or a cup?
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh eel KOH-noh oh lah kohp-PEHT-tah
responses.local: Vuole il cono o la coppetta?

responses.english: With whipped cream?
responses.phonetic: kohn PAH-nah
responses.local: Con panna?

phrases.intent_en: Order a large cone of gelato
phrases.cultural_note: Cones come in three sizes: piccolo (small), medio (medium), grande (large). The wafer cone ('cialda') is the default. In summer, expect a short wait — locals take gelato seriously.

likely_response.local: Che gusti vuole mettere nel cono?
likely_response.pronunciation: keh GOOS-tee vwoh-LEH meh-TEH-reh nehl KOH-noh?
likely_response.translation_en: Which flavors do you want in the cone?

responses.english: Which flavors do you want in the cone?
responses.phonetic: keh GOOS-tee WOH-leh meht-TEH-reh nehl KOH-noh
responses.local: Che gusti vuole mettere nel cono?

responses.english: The wafer cone is crispy, sorry if it breaks.
responses.phonetic: lah CHAL-dah eh FREHS-kah, mee SKOO-zee seh eh ROHT-tah
responses.local: La cialda è fresca, mi scusi se è rotta.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a gelato flavour recommendation
phrases.cultural_note: Italian gelato makers take enormous pride in their craft. In season: nocciola (hazelnut, very popular), pistacchio (bright green, intense), fragola (strawberry). Avoid neon-colored gelato — real fruit-based and chocolate-based gelato are muted in color.

likely_response.local: Il nostro pistacchio è di Bronte, siciliano, eccezionale.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel NOHS-troh pees-TAH-kyo eh dee BROHN-teh, see-chee-LYAH-noh, et-cheh-tsyo-NAH-leh
likely_response.translation_en: Our pistachio is from Bronte in Sicily, exceptional.

responses.english: Our pistachio is from Bronte in Sicily, exceptional.
responses.phonetic: eel NOHS-trohs pees-TAHK-kyoh eh dee BROHN-teh, see-cheh-LYAH-noh, eh-cheh-tsyah-NOH-lah
responses.local: Il nostro pistacchio è di Bronte, siciliano, eccezionale.

responses.english: For chocolate lovers, we recommend the 70% dark chocolate.
responses.phonetic: pehr kee AH-mah eel chohk-koh-LAH-toh, proh-poh-NYAH-moh eel fohn-DEHN-teh
responses.local: Per chi ama il cioccolato, proponiamo il fondente 70%.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there are dairy-free gelato flavours
phrases.cultural_note: Many gelaterias now offer lactose-free options. Sorbetti (sorbets) are naturally dairy-free — often made with fruit or chocolate. Always confirm with the staff — they know their recipes best.

likely_response.local: Certo, abbiamo diversi gusti di sorbetto, sono senza latte.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, ahb-BYAH-moh dee-VEHR-see GOOS-tee dee sor-BEH-toh, SOH-noh SEHN-tsah LAH-teh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, we have several sorbet flavors, they're dairy-free.

responses.english: Of course, we have several sorbet flavors, they're dairy-free.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, ahb-BYAH-moh dee-VEHR-see GOOS-tee dee sohr-BEHT-toh, SOH-noh SEHN-tsah LAHT-teh
responses.local: Certo, abbiamo diversi gusti di sorbetto, sono senza latte.

responses.english: The lemon sorbet is our best seller.
responses.phonetic: eel sohr-BEHT-toh ahl lee-MOH-neh eh eel NOHS-tro ploo vehn-DOO-toh
responses.local: Il sorbetto al limone è il nostro più venduto.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for the price of gelato
phrases.cultural_note: Prices at gelaterias are usually displayed on a signboard. If not, ask before ordering — prices vary widely between tourist-area gelaterias and local ones. A gelato in a tourist area can cost €5-8; a local spot is often €2-3.

likely_response.local: Due euro e cinquanta per il cono medio.
likely_response.pronunciation: DWEH EH-oo-roh eh cheen-KWAN-tah pehr eel KOH-noh MEH-dyoh
likely_response.translation_en: Two euros fifty for the medium cone.

responses.english: Two euros fifty for the medium cone.
responses.phonetic: DOO-eh EH-oo-roh eh cheen-KWAHN-tah pehr eel KOH-noh MEH-dyoh
responses.local: Due euro e cinquanta per il cono medio.

responses.english: It depends on how many flavors you choose.
responses.phonetic: dee-PEHN-deh dah KWAHN-tee GOOS-tee SHEH-lyeh
responses.local: Dipende da quanti gusti sceglie.

phrases.cultural_note: Always start with 'Scusi' (formal) or 'Scusa' (informal). Many Italians in tourist areas speak some English but appreciate you trying Italian first.

likely_response.local: Sì, parlo un po' d'inglese.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, PAR-loh oon poh deen-GLEH-zeh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, I speak a little English.

responses.english: Yes, I speak a little English.
responses.phonetic: see, PAR-loh oon poh deen-GLEH-zeh
responses.local: Sì, parlo un po' d'inglese.

responses.english: I'm sorry, I don't speak English.
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, nohn PAR-loh een-GLEH-zeh
responses.local: Mi dispiace, non parlo inglese.

responses.english: A little — what do you need?
responses.phonetic: oon po, KO-zah ah bee-ZOHG-nyoh
responses.local: Un po', cosa ha bisogno?

phrases.cultural_note: Follow up with 'Può ripetere più lentamente?' if you want them to try again more slowly.

likely_response.local: Vado più lentamente, ok?
likely_response.pronunciation: VAH-doh pyoo LEN-tah-MEN-teh, ohk?
likely_response.translation_en: I'll go more slowly, OK?

responses.english: I'll go more slowly, OK?
responses.phonetic: VAH-doh ploo lah-tehn-DAH-mehn-teh, oh-KAY
responses.local: Vado più lentamente, ok?

responses.english: Would you like me to write it down?
responses.phonetic: vwoh-leh keh leh SKREE-vah
responses.local: Vuole che le scriva?

responses.english: Shall I try in English?
responses.phonetic: PROH-voh een een-GLEH-zeh
responses.local: Provo in inglese?

phrases.cultural_note: In Italy, say 'il bagno' or 'i servizi' — not 'la toeletta'. Cafés usually let customers use theirs if you buy something. Always ask first.

likely_response.local: È in fondo a destra.
likely_response.pronunciation: eh een FON-doh ah DEHS-trah
likely_response.translation_en: It's at the back on the right.

responses.english: It's at the back on the right.
responses.phonetic: eh een FOHN-goh ah DEH-strah
responses.local: È in fondo a destra.

responses.english: Over there in the back, on the left.
responses.phonetic: lah een FOHN-goh, SOOL-lah see-NEE-strah
responses.local: Là in fondo, sulla sinistra.

responses.english: Sorry, the bathroom is only for customers.
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, eel BAHN-yoh EH SOH-loh pehr ee klee-EHN-tee
responses.local: Mi dispiace, il bagno è solo per i clienti.

phrases.cultural_note: These two phrases are essential. 'Grazie mille' is slightly more emphatic than 'grazie'. Italians highly value polite expressions — use them at every opportunity.

likely_response.local: Prego!
likely_response.pronunciation: PREH-goh!
likely_response.translation_en: You're welcome!

responses.english: You're welcome!
responses.phonetic: PREH-goh
responses.local: Prego!

responses.english: Not at all!
responses.phonetic: dee NOO-lah
responses.local: Di nulla!

responses.english: Don't mention it!
responses.phonetic: see FEE-goo-ree
responses.local: Si figuri!

phrases.cultural_note: Emergency in Italy: 112 (European emergency), 118 (medical), 113 (police), 115 (fire). All work from any phone without credit.

likely_response.local: Chiamo subito i soccorsi!
likely_response.pronunciation: KYAH-moh SOO-bee-toh ee SOHK-kor-see!
likely_response.translation_en: I'm calling for help right away!

responses.english: I'm calling for help right away!
responses.phonetic: KYAH-moh SOO-bee-toh ee sohk-KOR-see
responses.local: Chiamo subito i soccorsi!

responses.english: What happened?
responses.phonetic: KO-zah eh sook-KESS-oh
responses.local: Cosa è successo?

responses.english: Stay calm, I'm coming right now.
responses.phonetic: REH-stee KAL-moh, ah-RREE-voh SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Resti calmo, arrivo subito.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to a place
phrases.cultural_note: Italians often give directions with hand gestures and landmark references ('after the church, before the bar'). Don't expect precise street numbers.

likely_response.local: Vada sempre dritto, poi giri a sinistra.
likely_response.pronunciation: VAH-dah SEM-preh DREE-toh, PWEH-jee ah see-NEES-trah
likely_response.translation_en: Go straight, then turn left.

responses.english: Go straight, then turn left.
responses.phonetic: VAH-dah SEHM-preh DREET-toh, poh YEE-ree ah see-NEE-strah
responses.local: Vada sempre dritto, poi giri a sinistra.

responses.english: It's about a five-minute walk from here.
responses.phonetic: eh ah TSEE-rkah chee-KWEH mee-NOO-tee ah PYEH-dee dah koo-ee
responses.local: È a circa cinque minuti a piedi da qui.

responses.english: Take bus number twelve.
responses.phonetic: PREHN-dah low-toh-BOOS NOO-meh-roh DOH-dee-chee
responses.local: Prenda l'autobus numero dodici.

phrases.cultural_note: Use 'Buongiorno' until mid-afternoon, then switch to 'Buonasera'. Always greet when entering any shop — it's expected and warmly received.

responses.english: Good morning!
responses.phonetic: bwohn-JOR-noh
responses.local: Buongiorno!

responses.english: Good evening, welcome!
responses.phonetic: bwoh-nah-SEH-rah, behn-veh-NOO-toh
responses.local: Buonasera, benvenuto!

phrases.cultural_note: 'Arrivederci' is the standard goodbye. Informally 'Ciao' (chow) works between people of similar age. 'Buona giornata' (have a good day) is a warm parting phrase.

responses.english: Goodbye!
responses.phonetic: ahr-ree-veh-DEHR-chee
responses.local: Arrivederci!

responses.english: Bye, have a good day!
responses.phonetic: CHOW, BWOH-nah jor-NAH-tah
responses.local: Ciao, buona giornata!

phrases.cultural_note: 'Scusi' (formal) is used to get attention or squeeze past someone. 'Scusa' is the informal version. 'Mi dispiace' is a fuller apology.

responses.english: Don't worry about it.
responses.phonetic: nohn see preh-OH-koo-pee
responses.local: Non si preoccupi.

responses.english: No problem!
responses.phonetic: nehs-SOON proh-BLEH-mah
responses.local: Nessun problema!

phrases.cultural_note: Point at the item while asking if the name is tricky. Italians are generally happy to show you the price on a calculator or phone.

responses.english: It costs five euros.
responses.phonetic: KOH-stah CHEEN-kweh EH-oo-roh
responses.local: Costa cinque euro.

responses.english: The price is on the label.
responses.phonetic: eel PREHT-tsoh eh sool kar-tehl-LEE-noh
responses.local: Il prezzo è sul cartellino.

phrases.cultural_note: All-purpose location question. Show on your phone if you can't pronounce the name.

responses.english: It's there, at the back.
responses.phonetic: eh LEE, een FOHN-doh
responses.local: È lì, in fondo.

responses.english: I don't know, sorry.
responses.phonetic: nohn loh SOH, mee dees-PYAH-cheh
responses.local: Non lo so, mi dispiace.

phrases.cultural_note: Italians often use 'Esatto!' (exactly) to affirm strongly. 'Forse' is politely non-committal. A firm 'No, grazie' with a hand wave is a clear refusal.

responses.english: Agreed.
responses.phonetic: dah-KOR-doh
responses.local: D'accordo.

responses.english: Perfect.
responses.phonetic: pehr-FEHT-toh
responses.local: Perfetto.

phrases.cultural_note: Simple and widely understood. Follow with 'mi dispiace' (sorry) to be polite.

responses.english: I understand.
responses.phonetic: kah-PEES-koh
responses.local: Capisco.

responses.english: I'll try to find someone who knows.
responses.phonetic: PROH-voh ah troh-VAH-reh kwahl-KOO-noh keh SAH
responses.local: Provo a trovare qualcuno che sa.

phrases.intent_en: Ask someone to write it down
phrases.cultural_note: Very useful for names of streets, dishes, or medications. Italians will happily write things down or show you on their phone.

responses.english: Of course, here it is written.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, EH-koh-loh SKREE-toh
responses.local: Certo, eccolo scritto.

responses.english: I don't have a pen, I'll show you on my phone.
responses.phonetic: nohn oh OO-nah PEHN-nah, LYEH-loh MOHS-troh sool teh-LEH-foh-noh
responses.local: Non ho una penna, glielo mostro sul telefono.

phrases.intent_en: Say you have a booking under a name
phrases.text_local: Ho una prenotazione a nome di...
phrases.pronunciation: oh OO-nah preh-noh-tah-TSYOH-neh ah NOH-meh dee
phrases.cultural_note: Check-in in Italy is typically from 2pm (later than some countries). If you arrive before 2pm, your room may not be ready. Always confirm your reservation name as it's written — hotels may have multiple bookings with similar names.

likely_response.local: Sì, la trovo. Ha un documento con foto, per favore?
likely_response.pronunciation: see, lah TROH-voh. AH oon doh-koo-MEN-toh kon FOH-toh, pehr fah-VOH-reh?
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, I found it. Do you have a photo ID, please?

responses.english: Yes, I found it. Do you have a photo ID, please?
responses.phonetic: see, lah TROH-voh. ah oon doh-koo-MEHN-toh kohn FEE-toh, pehr pah-VOH-reh
responses.local: Sì, la trovo. Ha un documento con foto, per favore?

responses.english: I'm sorry, I can't find your reservation. Do you have the email confirmation?
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, nohn TROH-voh lah soo-AH preh-noh-tah-TSYOH-neh. ah lah kohn-FEHR-mah VEE-ah eh-MEHL
responses.local: Mi dispiace, non trovo la sua prenotazione. Ha la conferma via email?

phrases.cultural_note: Italian hotel breakfast ('colazione') is typically continental: coffee, pastries, bread, ham and cheese. It may or may not be included — always ask. In budget hotels ('pensione'), breakfast is often just coffee and bread.

likely_response.local: Sì, la colazione è inclusa. Il buffet è nella sala a sinistra, dalle sette alle dieci.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, lah koh-lah-tsyoh-NEH eh een-KLOO-zah. eel boo-FEH eh NEL-lah SAH-lah ah SEEN-ees-trah, DAHL-leh SET-teh AH-leh dee-EH-chee
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, breakfast is included. The buffet is in the room on the left, from seven to ten.

responses.english: Yes, breakfast is included. The buffet is in the room on the left, from seven to ten.
responses.phonetic: see, lah koh-lah-TSYOH-neh eh een-KLOO-zah. eel boo-FEH eh NEHL-lah SAH-lah ah see-NEE-strah, DAHL-leh SEHT-teh AHL-leh DYEH-chee
responses.local: Sì, la colazione è inclusa. Il buffet è nella sala a sinistra, dalle sette alle dieci.

responses.english: No, breakfast costs ten euros extra if you'd like it.
responses.phonetic: noh, lah koh-lah-TSYOH-neh KOH-stah DYEH-chee EH-oo-roh een ploo, seh deh-zee-DEH-rah
responses.local: No, la colazione costa dieci euro in più, se desidera.

phrases.intent_en: Report broken key or air conditioning
phrases.text_local: La chiave non funziona. / L'aria condizionata non funziona.
phrases.pronunciation: lah KYAH-veh nohn foon-TSYOH-nah. / lah-REE-ah kohn-dee-tsyoh-NAH-tah nohn foon-TSYOH-nah
phrases.cultural_note: Italian hotels (especially older ones) commonly have AC breakdowns, elevator issues, and plumbing problems. Don't hesitate to report issues — staff are used to dealing with old building systems. AC in Italy is typically individually controlled per room.

likely_response.local: Chiamo subito il tecnico. Le cambio stanza intanto.
likely_response.pronunciation: KYAH-moh SOO-bee-toh eel TEK-nee-koh. leh KAHM-byoh STAHN-tsah een-TAHN-toh
likely_response.translation_en: I'm calling the technician right now. I'll change your room in the meantime.

responses.english: I'm calling the technician right now. I'll change your room in the meantime.
responses.phonetic: KYAH-moh soo-BEE-toh eel TEH-knee-koh. leh KAHM-byoh STAH-tsah EEN-tahn-toh
responses.local: Chiamo subito il tecnico. Le cambio stanza intanto.

responses.english: Try resetting the air conditioning, there's a red button.
responses.phonetic: PROH-vee ah reh-seht-tah-REH-reh eel klee-mah-tee-tzah-TOH-reh, cheh oon pool-SAHN-teh ROHS-soh
responses.local: Provi a resettare il climatizzatore, c'è un pulsante rosso.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a taxi to be called
phrases.text_local: Può chiamarmi un taxi, per favore?
phrases.pronunciation: pwoh kyah-MAHR-mee oon TAHK-see, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: In Italy, taxis cannot be hailed on the street reliably in most cities — you call for one or have your hotel/restaurant call. In Rome and Milan there are taxi stands ('posto taxi') at major locations. Uber is limited to licensed drivers in most of Italy.

likely_response.local: Certo, le chiamo un taxi. Per dove deve andare?
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, leh KYAH-moh oon TAHK-see. pehr DOH-veh DEH-veh AHN-dah-reh?
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll call you a taxi. Where do you need to go?

responses.english: Of course, I'll call you a taxi. Where do you need to go?
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, leh KYAH-moh oon TAHK-see. pehr DOH-veh DEH-veh ahn-DAH-reh
responses.local: Certo, le chiamo un taxi. Per dove deve andare?

responses.english: The taxi will arrive in five minutes. Please stay at reception.
responses.phonetic: eel TAHK-see ah-RRAH-vah trah cheen-KWEH mee-NOO-tee. REH-stee een reh-sehp-SYOHN
responses.local: Il taxi arriva tra cinque minuti. Resti in reception.

phrases.text_local: Mi può dare la password del Wi-Fi?
phrases.pronunciation: mee pwoh dah-REH lah PAHSs-word dehl wee-FEE
phrases.cultural_note: Wi-Fi in Italian hotels varies widely in quality. In budget hotels it may be patchy or paid. Always ask the password ('password' is used in Italian too, or 'chiave Wi-Fi'). In older buildings, walls are thick and signal may be weak.

likely_response.local: Certo, la password è 'hotelroma2024', tutto attaccato.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, lah PAHS-sword eh oh-TEL-roh-mah-DUEH-mila-VEHN-tee-KWAH-troh, TOHL-toh ah-TAK-KAH-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, the password is 'hotelroma2024', all together.

responses.english: Of course, the password is 'hotelroma2024', all together.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, lah PAHSs-word eh oh-TEHL ROH-mah DOH-mee-kay vehn-tee-kwattro, TOOT-toh ahk-kahp-PAH-toh
responses.local: Certo, la password è 'hotelroma2024', tutto attaccato.

responses.english: Wi-Fi is included. Connect to the 'HotelMilan' network, the key is on your receipt.
responses.phonetic: eel wee-FEE eh een-KLOO-zoh. see koh-LEH-jee AHL-lah REH-teh ah-TEL-MEE-lahn, lah KYAH-veh eh een ree-cheh-VOO-tah
responses.local: Il Wi-Fi è incluso. Si colleghi alla rete 'HotelMilan', la chiave è in ricevuta.

phrases.text_local: Ho bisogno di un cuscino in più.
phrases.pronunciation: oh bee-ZOHG-nyoh dee oon koo-SHEE-noh een ploo
phrases.cultural_note: Italian pillows are often firmer than those in other countries. If you prefer a softer pillow, ask — most hotels have extras. Blankets are usually provided, but extra blankets in winter are available on request.

likely_response.local: Certo, glielo porto subito.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, YEH-loh POR-toh SOO-bee-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll bring it right up.

responses.english: Of course, I'll bring it right up.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, LYEH-loh POHR-toh soo-BEE-toh
responses.local: Certo, glielo porto subito.

responses.english: I'm sorry, we're out of extra pillows tonight. I'll lend you mine.
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, SYAH-moh SEHN-tsah koo-SHEE-nee een ploo stah-NOHT-teh. leh PREH-stoh eel NOHS-trohs
responses.local: Mi dispiace, siamo senza cuscini in più stanotte. Le presto il nostro.

phrases.cultural_note: Wake-up calls are standard at Italian hotels. Give a specific time — the front desk will call your room phone.

likely_response.local: Certo, la chiamo alle sette in punto.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, lah KYAH-moh AHL-leh SEHT-teh een POON-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll call you at seven sharp.

responses.english: Of course, I'll call you at seven sharp.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, lah KYAH-moh AHL-leh SEHT-teh een POON-toh
responses.local: Certo, la chiamo alle sette in punto.

responses.english: At what time exactly?
responses.phonetic: ah keh OH-rah eh-zaht-tah-MEHN-teh
responses.local: A che ora esattamente?

phrases.text_local: Può far pulire la camera oggi?
phrases.pronunciation: pwoh far poo-LEE-reh lah KAH-meh-rah OH-jee
phrases.cultural_note: Many Italian hotels skip daily cleaning unless requested. Hang the 'Rifare la camera' card or ask reception.

likely_response.local: Certo, passiamo nel pomeriggio.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, pahs-SYAH-moh nehl poh-meh-REE-joh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, we'll come in the afternoon.

responses.english: Of course, we'll come in the afternoon.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, pahs-SYAH-moh nehl poh-meh-REE-joh
responses.local: Certo, passiamo nel pomeriggio.

responses.english: The room will be ready by 2pm.
responses.phonetic: lah KAH-meh-rah sah-RAH PROHN-tah pehr leh kwaht-TOR-dee-chee
responses.local: La camera sarà pronta per le quattordici.

phrases.text_local: C'è molto rumore nella camera accanto.
phrases.pronunciation: cheh MOHL-toh roo-MOH-reh NEHL-lah KAH-meh-rah ahk-KAHN-toh
phrases.cultural_note: Report noise to reception — they can call the adjacent room. Italian hotels typically observe quiet hours after 11pm.

likely_response.local: Mi dispiace, provvediamo subito.
likely_response.pronunciation: mee dees-PYAH-cheh, proh-VEH-dyah-moh SOO-bee-toh
likely_response.translation_en: I'm sorry, we'll deal with it right away.

responses.english: I'm sorry, we'll deal with it right away.
responses.phonetic: mee dees-PYAH-cheh, proh-VEH-dyah-moh SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Mi dispiace, provvediamo subito.

responses.english: Would you like to change rooms?
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh kahm-BYAH-reh KAH-meh-rah
responses.local: Vuole cambiare camera?

phrases.text_local: La doccia non funziona. / Non c'è acqua calda.
phrases.pronunciation: lah DOH-chah nohn foon-TSYOH-nah. / nohn cheh AH-kwah KAHL-dah
phrases.cultural_note: Report issues promptly — Italian hotel maintenance is generally quick to respond. If unfixable, ask to change room.

likely_response.local: Mando subito qualcuno.
likely_response.pronunciation: MAHN-doh SOO-bee-toh kwahl-KOO-noh
likely_response.translation_en: I'll send someone right away.

responses.english: I'll send someone right away.
responses.phonetic: MAHN-doh SOO-bee-toh kwahl-KOO-noh
responses.local: Mando subito qualcuno.

responses.english: We can offer you another room.
responses.phonetic: leh proh-poh-NYAH-moh oon-AHL-trah KAH-meh-rah
responses.local: Le proponiamo un'altra camera.

phrases.text_local: Vorrei fare il check-out, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: vor-RAY FAH-reh eel CHEK-owt, pehr fah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Standard checkout in Italy is noon. Hand in your key at reception. Ask for a 'ricevuta' (receipt) if you need it for expenses.

likely_response.local: Certamente, Le preparo il conto.
likely_response.pronunciation: chehr-tah-MEHN-teh, leh preh-PAH-roh eel KOHN-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Certainly, I'll prepare your bill.

responses.english: Certainly, I'll prepare your bill.
responses.phonetic: chehr-tah-MEHN-teh, leh preh-PAH-roh eel KOHN-toh
responses.local: Certamente, Le preparo il conto.

responses.english: Did you use the minibar?
responses.phonetic: ah kohn-soo-MAH-toh kwahl-KOH-zah dahl mee-nee-BAR
responses.local: Ha consumato qualcosa dal minibar?

phrases.text_local: Potete custodire i bagagli fino a stasera?
phrases.pronunciation: poh-TEH-teh koos-toh-DEE-reh ee bah-GAH-lyee FEE-noh ah stah-SEH-rah
phrases.cultural_note: Most Italian hotels offer free luggage storage ('deposito bagagli') before check-in and after checkout. They'll give you a ticket.

likely_response.local: Certo, ecco il suo bigliettino.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, EH-koh eel SOO-oh beel-YEHT-tee-noh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, here's your ticket.

responses.english: Of course, here's your ticket.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, EH-koh eel SOO-oh beel-YEHT-tee-noh
responses.local: Certo, ecco il suo bigliettino.

responses.english: You can collect them whenever you like.
responses.phonetic: pwoh ree-tee-RAR-lee KWAHN-doh WOH-leh
responses.local: Può ritirarli quando vuole.

phrases.text_local: Un biglietto per la metropolitana, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: oon bee-LYEHT-toh pehr lah meh-troh-poh-lee-TAH-nah, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Metro tickets in Rome, Milan, and Naples are interchangeable with bus tickets — buy a single 'biglietto' for both. In Rome: Linea A (orange), Linea B (blue). In Milan: M1 (red), M2 (green), M3 (yellow). Tickets cost €1-1.50 depending on the city.

likely_response.local: Un biglietto o una tessera da dieci corse?
likely_response.pronunciation: oon bee-LYEHT-toh oh OO-nah TEHS-seh-rah dah dee-EH-chee KOR-seh?
likely_response.translation_en: A single ticket or a ten-ride card?

responses.english: A single ticket or a ten-ride card?
responses.phonetic: oon bee-LYEHT-toh oh OO-nah TEHS-seh-rah dah DYEH-chee KOHR-seh
responses.local: Un biglietto o una tessera da dieci corse?

responses.english: The ticket is valid for ninety minutes from the stamp, including the bus.
responses.phonetic: eel bee-LYEHT-toh VAH-leh noh-VAHN-tah mee-NOO-tee dahl TEHM-proh, kohm-PREHN-deh AHN-keh low-toh-BOOS
responses.local: Il biglietto vale novanta minuti dal timbro, comprende anche l'autobus.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if train goes to direction
phrases.cultural_note: Metro lines in Italy have clear terminus station names. Always confirm before boarding — Romans famously board the wrong metro going the opposite direction without realizing until several stops later.

likely_response.local: Sì, questo treno va verso Battistini.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, KWEH-stoh TREH-noh vah VEHR-soh bah-tee-STEE-nee
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, this train goes towards Battistini.

responses.english: Yes, this train goes towards Battistini.
responses.phonetic: see, KWES-toh TREH-noh vah VEHR-soh baht-tee-STEE-nee
responses.local: Sì, questo treno va verso Battistini.

responses.english: No, for that direction take the next platform.
responses.phonetic: noh, pehr KWEL-lah dee-reh-TSYOH-neh PREHN-dah eel bee-NAH-ryoh ahk-KAHN-toh
responses.local: No, per quella direzione prenda il binario accanto.

phrases.cultural_note: Metro announcements in Italy are often in Italian only, sometimes with English. If you're not sure where you are, ask — Italians are usually happy to help.

likely_response.local: La prossima fermata è Spagna.
likely_response.pronunciation: lah PROS-see-mah fehr-MAH-tah eh SPAH-nyah
likely_response.translation_en: The next stop is Spagna.

responses.english: The next stop is Spagna.
responses.phonetic: lah PROHS-see-mah fehr-MAH-tah EH SPAHN-yah
responses.local: La prossima fermata è Spagna.

responses.english: In two stops, get off at Flaminio.
responses.phonetic: trah DOO-eh fehr-MAH-teh SHEHN-deh ah flah-MEE-nyoh
responses.local: Tra due fermate scende a Flaminio.

phrases.cultural_note: 'Uscita' = exit. This is critical in big metro stations like Roma Termini or Milano Centrale where exits can be far from each other. Follow the 'USCITA' signs — they're always clearly marked.

likely_response.local: L'uscita è a sinistra, dopo le scale mobili.
likely_response.pronunciation: LOO-shee-tah eh ah SEE-nes-trah, AH-peh-reh leh SKAH-leh MOH-bee-leh
likely_response.translation_en: The exit is on the left, after the escalators.

responses.english: The exit is on the left, after the escalators.
responses.phonetic: loo-SHEE-tah eh ah see-NEE-strah, DOH-pah leh SKAH-leh MOH-bee-lee
responses.local: L'uscita è a sinistra, dopo le scale mobili.

responses.english: There's an exit right here in front, and another on the opposite platform.
responses.phonetic: cheh oon-loo-SHEE-tah koo-ee dah-VAHN-tee, eh oon-AHL-trah ahl bee-NAH-ryoh op-POH-stoh
responses.local: C'è un'uscita qui davanti, e un'altra al binario opposto.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to get off metro
phrases.text_local: Scusi, mi può aiutare? Non so dove scendere.
phrases.pronunciation: SKOO-zee, mee pwoh ah-yoo-TAH-reh, nohn soh DOH-veh SHEHN-deh-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Metro staff in Italy are often wear uniforms and can be found at information points ('punto informazioni'). In smaller stations, there may be no staff — in that case, ask other passengers who are waiting for the train.

likely_response.local: Certo! Deve scendere alla prossima, poi prendere la linea arancione.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh! DEH-veh shehn-DEH-reh AHL-lah PROS-see-mah, poy prehn-DEH-reh lah LEE-neh-ah ah-rahn-CHOH-neh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course! You need to get off at the next stop, then take the orange line.

responses.english: Of course! You need to get off at the next stop, then take the orange line.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, DEH-veh SHEHN-deh-reh AHL-lah PROHS-see-mah, poh PREHN-deh-reh lah LEE-neh-ah ah-rahn-CHOH-neh
responses.local: Certo! Deve scendere alla prossima, poi prendere la linea arancione.

responses.english: Get on the train and get off at the third stop after this one.
responses.phonetic: SAHL-gah sool TREH-noh eh SHEHN-dah AHL-lah TEHR-tsah fehr-MAH-tah DOH-pah KWES-tah
responses.local: Salga sul treno e scenda alla terza fermata dopo questa.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to change / transfer trains
phrases.text_local: Dov'è il cambio per la linea…?
phrases.pronunciation: doh-VEH eel KAHM-byoh pehr lah LEE-neh-ah
phrases.cultural_note: Rome's metro has only 3 lines (A, B, C) with limited interchange. Milan's metro is more extensive with 5 lines. Follow the signs for 'corrispondenza' or 'cambio' — they're usually colour-coded by line.

responses.english: The transfer is at Termini.
responses.phonetic: eel KAHM-byoh eh ah tehr-MEE-nee
responses.local: Il cambio è a Termini.

responses.english: Follow the arrows for line B.
responses.phonetic: SEH-gwah leh FREH-cheh pehr lah LEE-neh-ah BEE
responses.local: Segua le frecce per la linea B.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about a day pass or tourist card
phrases.text_local: C'è un biglietto giornaliero o turistico?
phrases.pronunciation: cheh oon beel-YEHT-toh jor-nah-LYEH-roh oh too-REES-tee-koh
phrases.cultural_note: Rome offers 24h, 48h, and 72h tourist passes covering metro, bus, and tram. Milan has similar integrated passes. Buy at ticket machines (ATM) or at the information desk. Validate before boarding — inspectors check regularly.

responses.english: Yes, the day pass costs seven euros.
responses.phonetic: SEE, eel beel-YEHT-toh jor-nah-LYEH-roh KOH-stah SEHT-teh EH-oo-roh
responses.local: Sì, il biglietto giornaliero costa sette euro.

responses.english: You can buy it at the ticket machine.
responses.phonetic: pwoh kohm-PRAR-loh AHL-lah MAH-kee-nah ow-toh-MAH-tee-kah
responses.local: Può comprarlo alla macchina automatica.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how many stops to your destination
