phrases.cultural_note: A simple and universally understood question. Show the stop name on your phone map if the pronunciation is difficult. Other passengers are generally happy to help count stops on the map.

responses.english: Four more stops.
responses.phonetic: MAHN-kah-noh KWAHT-troh fehr-MAH-teh
responses.local: Mancano quattro fermate.

responses.english: You need to get off at the next stop.
responses.phonetic: DEH-veh SHEHN-deh-reh AHL-lah PROHS-see-mah
responses.local: Deve scendere alla prossima.

phrases.intent_en: Ask when the last train runs
phrases.text_local: A che ora è l'ultimo treno?
phrases.pronunciation: ah keh OH-rah eh LOOL-tee-moh TREH-noh
phrases.cultural_note: Rome's metro closes at 11:30pm (01:30am Friday/Saturday). Milan's runs until about midnight (01:30am weekends). After the last metro, night buses ('notturni') take over — check the route maps at the station.

responses.english: The last train is at 11:30.
responses.phonetic: LOOL-tee-moh TREH-noh eh AHL-leh OON-dee-chee eh TREHN-tah
responses.local: L'ultimo treno è alle undici e trenta.

responses.english: At weekends the service is extended.
responses.phonetic: nay wee-KEHND eel sehr-VEE-tsyoh eh proh-loon-GAH-toh
responses.local: Nei weekend il servizio è prolungato.

phrases.cultural_note: Italian metro stations often have just one or two exits. Show the destination on your phone — staff at the barrier are usually helpful. In Rome, the Colosseo stop has clearly signed exits directly facing the monument.

responses.english: Exit A is closest to the Colosseum.
responses.phonetic: loo-SHEE-tah AH eh lah pyoo vee-CHEE-nah ahl koh-lohs-SEH-oh
responses.local: L'uscita A è la più vicina al Colosseo.

responses.english: Follow the signs at the exit.
responses.phonetic: SEH-gwah leh een-dee-kah-TSYOH-nee ahl-loo-SHEE-tah
responses.local: Segua le indicazioni all'uscita.

phrases.text_local: Vorrei un cornetto e un caffè, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: vohr-RAY oon kohr-NEHT-toh eh oon kah-FEH, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Pasticcerias are the Italian breakfast spot: a coffee and a pastry at the bar costs €2-3 total. Cornetti come plain or filled (jam, Nutella, custard). In Naples, 'sfogliatelle' (layered ricotta pastry) and 'babà' (rum-soaked sponge cake) are the local specialties.

likely_response.local: Il cornetto alla crema o alla marmellata?
likely_response.pronunciation: eel kor-NEHT-toh AHL-lah KREH-mah oh AHL-lah mar-mehl-LAH-tah
likely_response.translation_en: The croissant with cream or jam?

responses.english: The croissant with cream or jam?
responses.phonetic: eel kohr-NEHT-toh AHL-lah KREH-mah oh AHL-lah mahr-meh-LAH-tah
responses.local: Il cornetto alla crema o alla marmellata?

responses.english: Do you have a preference for the coffee? Macchiato, corretto, decaf?
responses.phonetic: ah preh-feh-REHN-tsah pehr eel kah-FEH, mahk-KYAH-toh, koh-REHT-toh, deh-kah-feh-ee-NAH-toh
responses.local: Ha preferenza per il caffè? Macchiato, corretto, decaffeinato?

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a gift box of pastries
phrases.text_local: Vorrei una scatola di pasticcini per regalo.
phrases.pronunciation: vohr-RAY OO-nah SKAH-toh-lah dee pahs-tee-CHEE-nee pehr reh-GAH-loh
phrases.cultural_note: Pasticcerias sell 'scatole di pasticcini' — gift boxes of assorted pastries. Perfect for bringing to hosts when invited to an Italian home. Order a day ahead for larger quantities.

likely_response.local: Certo! Quanti ne vuole? Abbiamo confezioni da sei, dodici o ventiquattro.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh! KWAHN-tee neh VWOH-leh? AB-byah-moh kon-feh-tsyoh-nee dah see, DOH-dee-cheh oh ven-tee-KWAH-troh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course! How many do you want? We have boxes of six, twelve, or twenty-four.

responses.english: Of course! How many do you want? We have boxes of six, twelve, or twenty-four.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, KWAHN-tee neh WOH-leh, ahb-BYAH-moh kohn-feh-TSYOH-nee dah seh-ee, DOH-dee-chee oh vehn-tee-KWAHT-troh
responses.local: Certo! Quanti ne vuole? Abbiamo confezioni da sei, dodici o ventiquattro.

responses.english: I'll prepare a mixed selection for you. Would you like a greeting card?
responses.phonetic: leh preh-PRAH-oh OO-nah seh-leht-TSYOH-neh MEES-tah. deh-ZEE-deh-rah eel BEE-lyeh-toh dee ow-GOO-ree
responses.local: Le preparo una selezione mista. Desidera il biglietto di auguri?

phrases.intent_en: Ask if Sicilian cassata is available
phrases.cultural_note: Cassata Siciliana is a iconic Sicilian cake: ricotta-filled sponge cake decorated with royal icing and candied fruits. Primarily available in Sicily and Southern Italy, but found in pasticcerias throughout Italy. Usually made for Easter and special occasions.

likely_response.local: Sì, l'abbiamo fresca di oggi. Vuole una fetta o tutta la torta?
likely_response.pronunciation: see, LAHB-byah-moh FREH-skah dee OH-jee. VWOH-leh OO-nah FEHT-sah oh TOOT-tah lah TOHR-tah
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, we have it fresh from today. Would you like a slice or the whole cake?

responses.english: Yes, we have it fresh from today. Would you like a slice or the whole cake?
responses.phonetic: see, lahb-BYAH-moh FREHS-kah dee OH-jee. WOH-leh OO-nah FEHT-tah oh TOOT-tah lah TOHR-tah
responses.local: Sì, l'abbiamo fresca di oggi. Vuole una fetta o tutta la torta?

responses.english: We make it only on order, you can come back tomorrow.
responses.phonetic: lah fah-CHAH-moh SOH-loh soo ohr-dee-nah-TSYOH-neh, pwoh tohr-NAH-reh doh-MAH-nee
responses.local: La facciamo solo su ordinazione, può tornare domani.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to pay for coffee and croissant
phrases.text_local: Vorrei un caffè e un cornetto. Mi siedo qui o pago alla cassa?
phrases.pronunciation: vohr-RAY oon kah-FEH eh oon kohr-NEHT-toh. mee SYEH-doh kee oh PAH-goh AHL-lah KAHS-sah
phrases.cultural_note: In Italian pasticcerias, there's almost always a cheaper 'bar' price (standing at the counter) and a more expensive 'tavolo' or 'sala' price (seated). The bar price is typically €1-2 for coffee vs €3-5 seated.

likely_response.local: Se si siede al bancone, il caffè costa un euro. Se preferisce sedersi, le porto tutto.
likely_response.pronunciation: seh see SYEH-deh ahl bahn-KOH-neh, eel kaf-FEH KOH-stah oon YOO-roh. seh preh-FEH-ree-seh SYEH-deh-ree, leh POHR-toh TOOT-toh
likely_response.translation_en: If you stand at the counter, coffee is one euro. If you'd prefer to sit, I'll bring everything to you.

responses.english: If you stand at the counter, coffee is one euro. If you'd prefer to sit, I'll bring everything to you.
responses.phonetic: seh see SYEH-deh ahl bahn-KOH-neh, eel kah-FEH KOH-stah oon EH-oo-roh. seh preh-feh-REE-seh seh-DEHR-see, leh POHR-toh TOOT-toh
responses.local: Se si siede al bancone, il caffè costa un euro. Se preferisce sedersi, le porto tutto.

responses.english: I'll pay at the register and sit down, thank you.
responses.phonetic: PAH-goh AHL-lah KAHS-sah ee mee SYEH-doh, GRAH-tsyeh
responses.local: Pago alla cassa e mi siedo, grazie.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what seasonal pastries are available
phrases.cultural_note: Italian pasticcerias change their offerings seasonally. Spring: colomba (Easter dove cake), Summer: fresh fruit tarts, Autumn: pastries with chestnuts and figs, Winter: panettone and pandoro for Christmas. If you see something you like, buy it — seasonal items sell out.

likely_response.local: Oggi abbiamo la crostata diamarene, stagionale. In più c'è la cheesecake alla siciliana.
likely_response.pronunciation: ohj-DJEE AB-byah-moh lah krohs-TAH-tah dee-ah-mah-REH-neh, stah-joh-NAH-leh. een pyoo CHEH lah kees-KEHK ah-lah see-chee-LYAH-nah
likely_response.translation_en: Today we have an apricot tart, seasonal. Plus a Sicilian-style cheesecake.

responses.english: Today we have an apricot tart, seasonal. Plus a Sicilian-style cheesecake.
responses.phonetic: OH-jee ahb-BYAH-moh lah krohs-TAH-tah dee ah-mah-RAY-neh, stah-joh-NAH-leh. een ploo cheh lah cheese-KEHK ah lah see-cheh-LYAH-nah
responses.local: Oggi abbiamo la crostata diamarene, stagionale. In più c'è la cheesecake alla siciliana.

responses.english: It's panettone season, would you like to try our artisan one?
responses.phonetic: SYAH-moh een peh-REE-oh-doh dee pah-neh-TOH-neh, WOH-leh proh-VAH-reh eel NOHS-trohr ahr-tee-jah-nah-LEH
responses.local: Siamo in periodo di panettone, vuole provare il nostro artigianale?

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the nearest pharmacy is
phrases.cultural_note: Italian pharmacies are marked with a green cross (not red like in many other countries). They're the first stop for minor health issues. 'Farmacia di turno' = the pharmacy that's open late or on Sundays. Every town has a rotating on-duty pharmacy schedule.

likely_response.local: La farmacia più vicina è in Via Roma, a due isolati da qui.
likely_response.pronunciation: lah far-mah-CHEE-ah pyoo VEE-chee-nah eh een VEE-ah ROH-mah, ah DOO-eh ee-SOH-la-tee dah KWEE
likely_response.translation_en: The nearest pharmacy is on Via Roma, two blocks from here.

responses.english: The nearest pharmacy is on Via Roma, two blocks from here.
responses.phonetic: lah fahr-mah-CHEE-ah ploo vee-CHEE-nah eh een VEE-ah ROH-mah, ah DOO-eh ee-zoh-LAH-tee dah KOO-ee
responses.local: La farmacia più vicina è in Via Roma, a due isolati da qui.

responses.english: The on-duty pharmacy today is at Piazza Cavour, [phone number].
responses.phonetic: lah fahr-mah-CHEE-ah dee TOHR-noh OH-jee eh een PYAH-tsah KAH-vohr
responses.local: La farmacia di turno oggi è in Piazza Cavour,电话号码.

phrases.text_local: Ho bisogno di qualcosa per il mal di testa.
phrases.pronunciation: oh bee-ZOHG-nyoh dee kwahl-KOH-zah pehr eel mahl dee TEHS-tah
phrases.cultural_note: Pharmacists in Italy are highly trained and can advise on mild conditions without a prescription. They often suggest specific Italian brands. Common OTC items: okcox (ibuprofen), Oki (ketoprofen), Tachipirina (paracetamol).

likely_response.local: Le推荐o questo antinfiammatorio, molto efficace.
likely_response.pronunciation: leh kon-SYEH-lyoh KWEH-stoh an-teen-fyam-mah-TOH-ree-oh, mohl-toh ef-feh-KAH-cheh
likely_response.translation_en: I recommend this anti-inflammatory, very effective.

responses.english: I recommend this anti-inflammatory, very effective.
responses.phonetic: leh ree-ko-mahn-DAH-yoh KWES-toh ahn-teen-fahm-mah-T OH-ree-oh, MOHL-toh eh-fee-KAH-cheh
responses.local: Le推荐o questo antinfiammatorio, molto efficace.

responses.english: Do you need something stronger? I need to ask the pharmacist.
responses.phonetic: ah bee-ZOHG-nyoh dee kwahl-KOH-zah dee ploo FOHR-teh, DEH-voh KYEH-deh-reh ahl fahr-mah-CHEE-stah
responses.local: Ha bisogno di qualcosa di più forte? Devo chiedere al farmacista.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a prescription is needed
phrases.cultural_note: Many medicines that are OTC in other countries require a prescription ('ricetta medica') in Italy — especially antibiotics ('antibiotici'), some painkillers, and certain cold medicines. The pharmacist can tell you if a doctor's visit is needed.

likely_response.local: Sì, per questo serve la ricetta del medico. Ha un dottore di fiducia?
likely_response.pronunciation: see, pehr KWEH-stoh SER-veh lah ree-CHEH-tah del MEH-dee-koh. AH oon dot-TOH-reh dee fee-DOO-tsah
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, this requires a doctor's prescription. Do you have a trusted doctor?

responses.english: Yes, this requires a doctor's prescription. Do you have a trusted doctor?
responses.phonetic: see, pehr KWES-toh SEHR-veh lah ree-CHEHT-tah dehl MEH-dee-koh. ah oon DOHT-toh-reh dee fee-DOO-chah
responses.local: Sì, per questo serve la ricetta del medico. Ha un dottore di fiducia?

responses.english: No, this is available without a prescription, you can buy it freely.
responses.phonetic: noh, KWES-toh eh SEHN-tsah oh-BLEE-goh dee ree-CHEHT-tah, lo pwoh kohm-PRAH-reh lee-beh-reh-MEHN-teh
responses.local: No, questo è senza obbligo di ricetta, lo può comprare liberamente.

phrases.text_local: Sono allergico agli antibiotici / al lattosio.
phrases.pronunciation: SOH-noh ah-LEHR-jee-koh AHL-yee ahn-tee-bee-OH-tee-chee / ahl laht-TOH-zee-oh
phrases.cultural_note: Always tell the pharmacist if you have allergies — especially to penicillin-type antibiotics, which are commonly prescribed in Italy. Italian packaging often lists active ingredients in Italian too — learn to read 'principio attivo' (active ingredient).

likely_response.local: Certo, le do un antibiotico senza penicillina. Ha anche allergie ad altri farmaci?
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, leh doh oon an-tee-byoh-TEE-koh SEN-tsah peh-nee-chee-LEE-nah. AH AN-kheh al-LEHR-jee-ah ad AL-tree far-MAH-chee
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll give you an antibiotic without penicillin. Are you also allergic to other medicines?

responses.english: Of course, I'll give you an antibiotic without penicillin. Are you also allergic to other medicines?
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, leh doh oon ahn-tee-bee-OH-tee-koh SEHN-tsah peh-nee-chee-LEE-nah. ah AHN-keh ah-LEHR-jee-eh ahl-TREE FAHR-mah-chee
responses.local: Certo, le do un antibiotico senza penicillina. Ha anche allergie ad altri farmaci?

responses.english: This product is gluten-free and lactose-free, it's fine for you.
responses.phonetic: KWES-toh proh-DOHT-toh eh SEHN-tsah GLOO-tee-neh eh SEHN-tsah laht-TOH-zee-oh, vah BEH-neh pehr LAY-ee
responses.local: Questo prodotto è senza glutine e senza lattosio, va bene per lei.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the nearest hospital is
phrases.cultural_note: In a medical emergency in Italy, call 118 (medical emergency) or go to the nearest 'pronto soccorso' (emergency room). EU citizens with EHIC cards are entitled to reduced-cost emergency treatment. Non-EU visitors should have travel insurance.

likely_response.local: L'ospedale più vicino è a cinque chilometri, chiamo un'ambulanza per lei.
likely_response.pronunciation: lohs-peh-DAH-leh pyoo VEE-chee-noh eh ah CHEEN-kweh kee-LOH-meh-tree, KYA-moh oon-am-boo-LAHN-tsah pehr LEH-ee
likely_response.translation_en: The nearest hospital is five kilometers away, I'm calling an ambulance for you.

responses.english: The nearest hospital is five kilometers away, I'm calling an ambulance for you.
responses.phonetic: lohs-peh-DAH-leh ploo vee-CHEE-noh eh ah CHEEN-kweh kee-LOH-meh-tree, KYAH-moh oon-ahm-boo-LAHN-tsah pehr LAY-ee
responses.local: L'ospedale più vicino è a cinque chilometri, chiamo un'ambulanza per lei.

responses.english: There's an emergency room on Via Turati, three blocks from here.
responses.phonetic: cheh oon PROHN-toh sohk-KOHR-soh ah VEE-ah too-RAH-tee, treh ee-zoh-LAH-tee dah KOO-ee
responses.local: C'è un pronto soccorso a via Turati, tre isolati da qui.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for mosquito repellent or sunscreen
phrases.text_local: Ha un repellente per zanzare? / Ha una protezione solare?
phrases.pronunciation: ah oon reh-pehl-LEHN-teh pehr tsahn-DZAH-reh / ah OO-nah proh-teh-tsyoh-NEH soh-LAH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Italian pharmacies stock quality sunscreen and insect repellent — especially important in summer in Italy where mosquitoes ('zanzare') can be aggressive in the Veneto and Po Valley regions. Pharmacist brands are often better than tourist-area shops.

likely_response.local: Certo, questo antizanzare è molto efficace, da applicare ogni due ore.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, KWEH-stoh an-tee-tsan-TSAH-reh eh MOHL-toh ef-fee-KAH-cheh, dah ap-plee-KAH-reh OHG-nee DWEH OH-reh.
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, this mosquito repellent is very effective, apply every two hours.

responses.english: Of course, this mosquito repellent is very effective, apply every two hours.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, KWES-toh ahn-tee-tsahn-DZAH-reh EH MOHL-toh eh-fee-KAH-cheh, dah ahp-plyee-KAH-reh OHG-nee DOO-eh OH-reh
responses.local: Certo, questo antizanzare è molto efficace, da applicare ogni due ore.

responses.english: I also have SPF 50 sunscreen, water resistant.
responses.phonetic: oh AHN-keh OO-nah proh-teh-tsyoh-NEH soh-LAH-reh cheen-KWAHN-tah, reh-ee-STEHN-teh ahl-LAH-kwah
responses.local: Ho anche una protezione solare fifty, resistente all'acqua.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for something for a cold or flu
phrases.text_local: Ho il raffreddore / l'influenza. Cosa mi consiglia?
phrases.pronunciation: oh eel rahf-fred-DOH-reh / leen-floo-EHN-tsah. KOH-zah mee kohn-SEE-lyah
phrases.cultural_note: Common Italian cold remedies: Tachifludec, Neoborocillina, Rinazina. Pharmacists will ask about specific symptoms before recommending.

likely_response.local: Le consiglio questo sciroppo e queste compresse.
likely_response.pronunciation: leh kohn-SEE-lyoh KWES-toh shee-ROHP-poh eh KWES-teh kohm-PREHS-seh
likely_response.translation_en: I recommend this syrup and these tablets.

responses.english: I recommend this syrup and these tablets.
responses.phonetic: leh kohn-SEE-lyoh KWEHS-toh shee-ROHP-poh eh KWEHS-teh kohm-PREHS-seh
responses.local: Le consiglio questo sciroppo e queste compresse.

responses.english: Do you have a fever?
responses.phonetic: ah lah FEHB-breh
responses.local: Ha la febbre?

phrases.intent_en: Ask for something for a fever
phrases.cultural_note: Paracetamol (Tachipirina) is the first choice in Italy. Ibuprofen (Moment, Brufen) is also common. Aspirin is used but less routinely for fever.

likely_response.local: Prenda la Tachipirina ogni sei ore.
likely_response.pronunciation: PREHN-dah lah tah-kee-pee-REE-nah OH-nyee SAY OH-reh
likely_response.translation_en: Take Tachipirina every six hours.

responses.english: Take Tachipirina every six hours.
responses.phonetic: PREHN-dah lah tah-kee-pee-REE-nah OH-nyee SAY OH-reh
responses.local: Prenda la Tachipirina ogni sei ore.

responses.english: What's your temperature?
responses.phonetic: KWAHN-tee GRAH-dee ah
responses.local: Quanti gradi ha?

phrases.intent_en: Ask for something for diarrhoea or nausea
phrases.text_local: Ho la diarrea / la nausea. Cosa mi consiglia?
phrases.pronunciation: oh lah dyar-REH-ah / lah NOW-seh-ah. KOH-zah mee kohn-SEE-lyah
phrases.cultural_note: For diarrhoea: Imodium (loperamide) or Dissenten. For nausea: Plasil (metoclopramide). Keep hydrated — 'sali di reidratazione' (rehydration salts) are sold at all pharmacies.

likely_response.local: Le consiglio l'Imodium e molta acqua.
likely_response.pronunciation: leh kohn-SEE-lyoh lee-MOH-dyoom eh MOHL-tah AH-kwah
likely_response.translation_en: I recommend Imodium and plenty of water.

responses.english: I recommend Imodium and plenty of water.
responses.phonetic: leh kohn-SEE-lyoh lee-MOH-dyoom eh MOHL-tah AH-kwah
responses.local: Le consiglio l'Imodium e molta acqua.

responses.english: How long have you had these symptoms?
responses.phonetic: dah KWAHN-toh TEHM-poh ah KWEHS-tee SEEN-toh-mee
responses.local: Da quanto tempo ha questi sintomi?

phrases.text_local: Ho bisogno di cerotti e disinfettante.
phrases.pronunciation: oh bee-ZOHN-nyoh dee cheh-ROHT-tee eh dee-zeen-feht-TAHN-teh
phrases.cultural_note: Italian pharmacies stock full wound care. Common items: Betadine or Amuchina (antiseptic), cerotti (plasters), garza (gauze), fascia elastica (bandage).

likely_response.local: Ecco quello che le serve.
likely_response.pronunciation: EH-koh KWEL-loh keh leh SEHR-veh
likely_response.translation_en: Here's what you need.

responses.english: Here's what you need.
responses.phonetic: EH-koh KWEL-loh keh leh SEHR-veh
responses.local: Ecco quello che le serve.

responses.english: Is the wound deep?
responses.phonetic: lah feh-REE-tah eh proh-FOHN-dah
responses.local: La ferita è profonda?

phrases.text_local: Ho una reazione allergica. Avete un antistaminico?
phrases.pronunciation: oh OO-nah reh-ah-TSYOH-neh ahl-LEHR-jee-kah. ah-VEH-teh oon ahn-tee-stah-MEE-nee-koh
phrases.cultural_note: Common antihistamines in Italy: Zyrtec (cetirizina), Loratadina, Clarityn. Pharmacists can advise on non-drowsy options ('non soporiferi').

likely_response.local: Le do lo Zyrtec, una compressa al giorno.
likely_response.pronunciation: leh doh loh ZEER-tek, OO-nah kohm-PREHS-sah ahl JHOHR-noh
likely_response.translation_en: I'll give you Zyrtec, one tablet per day.

responses.english: I'll give you Zyrtec, one tablet per day.
responses.phonetic: leh doh loh ZEER-tek, OO-nah kohm-PREHS-sah ahl JHOHR-noh
responses.local: Le do lo Zyrtec, una compressa al giorno.

responses.english: What are your symptoms?
responses.phonetic: KWAH-lee SOH-noh ee SOO-oy SEEN-toh-mee
responses.local: Quali sono i suoi sintomi?

phrases.intent_en: Ask how to take a medicine
phrases.cultural_note: Italian pharmacists always explain dosage. Key words: 'compressa' (tablet), 'capsula' (capsule), 'volte al giorno' (times a day), 'a stomaco pieno' (with food), 'con acqua' (with water).

likely_response.local: Una compressa tre volte al giorno, con acqua.
likely_response.pronunciation: OO-nah kohm-PREHS-sah TREH VOHL-teh ahl JHOHR-noh, kohn AH-kwah
likely_response.translation_en: One tablet three times a day, with water.

responses.english: One tablet three times a day, with water.
responses.phonetic: OO-nah kohm-PREHS-sah TREH VOHL-teh ahl JHOHR-noh, kohn AH-kwah
responses.local: Una compressa tre volte al giorno, con acqua.

responses.english: Take it with meals.
responses.phonetic: dah PREHN-deh-reh doo-RAHN-teh ee PAHS-tee
responses.local: Da prendere durante i pasti.

phrases.cultural_note: Pizza Margherita (tomato, mozzarella, basil) is the classic Neapolitan pizza. 'Marinara' (tomato, garlic, oregano) is for purists. Pizzas in Italy are smaller than American slices — one is usually a full meal. Waiters may ask 'per qui o da portare via?' (to eat here or take away).

likely_response.local: Ha preference di impasto? Normale o integrale?
likely_response.pronunciation: ah preh-feh-REN-tsah dee eem-PAH-stoh? nor-MAH-leh oh een-teh-GRAH-leh?
likely_response.translation_en: Do you prefer regular or whole wheat dough?

responses.english: Do you prefer regular or whole wheat dough?
responses.phonetic: ah preh-feh-REHN-tsee dee EEM-pahs-toh, nohr-MAH-leh oh een-teh-GRAH-leh
responses.local: Ha preference di impasto? Normale o integrale?

responses.english: The pizza will be ready in ten minutes.
responses.phonetic: lah PEET-sah eh PRAHN-tah een DYEH-chee mee-NOO-tee
responses.local: La pizza è pronta in dieci minuti.

responses.english: Would you also like something to drink?
responses.phonetic: WOH-leh AHN-keh kwahl-KOH-zah dah BEH-reh
responses.local: Vuole anche qualcosa da bere?

phrases.text_local: Una pizza al taglio, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: OO-nah PEET-tsah ahl TAH-lyoh, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Pizza al taglio is sold by weight at window shops — you point at what you want and pay by the slice. Found everywhere in Italy, especially good in Rome. Eaten standing or take-away. The pizza is often sold at room temperature or slightly warm — this is normal.

likely_response.local: Guarda cosa c'è oggi, indica quella che preferisce.
likely_response.pronunciation: GWAHR-dah KOH-zah cheh OH-jee, een-DEE-kah KWEL-lah keh preh-feh-REE-sheh
likely_response.translation_en: Look what's available today, point to the one you prefer.

responses.english: Look what's available today, point to the one you prefer.
responses.phonetic: GWAHR-dah KO-zah CHEH OH-jee, een-DEE-kah KWEL-lah keh preh-feh-REE-sheh
responses.local: Guarda cosa c'è oggi, indica quella che preferisce.

responses.english: How much do you want? One hundred grams, two hundred grams?
responses.phonetic: KWAHN-toh lah VOH-leh, oon EHT-toh, DOO-eh EHT-tee
responses.local: Quanto la vuole? Un etto, due etti?

phrases.text_local: Posso avere la pizza senza formaggio?
phrases.pronunciation: POHS-soh ah-VEH-reh lah PEET-sah SEHN-tsah for-MAH-joh
phrases.cultural_note: In Naples, 'pizza senza formaggio' is a valid request (marinara is the classic no-cheese option). Some restaurants resist this — they're proud of their mozzarella di bufala. But they will accommodate it.

likely_response.local: Certo, le faccio una marinara.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, leh FAH-choh OO-nah mah-ree-NAH-rah
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll make you a marinara.

responses.english: Of course, I'll make you a marinara.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, leh FAH-choh OO-nah mah-ree-NAH-rah
responses.local: Certo, le faccio una marinara.

responses.english: Without mozzarella or without all dairy? Buffalo mozzarella is a fresh cheese.
responses.phonetic: SEHN-tsah moh-tzah-REHL-lah oh SEHN-tsah laht-tee-CHEE-nee
responses.local: Senza mozzarella o senza latticini? La mozzarella di bufala è un formaggio fresco.

phrases.cultural_note: Italians are proud of their local pizzas. In Naples: Margherita, Marinara, or Pizza Napoletana (with anchovies). In Rome: Pizza Romana (thin, crispy). In Sicily: Pizza Siciliana (with anchovies, olives, capers).

likely_response.local: La nostra pizza con i ventricola è ottima, la consiglio.
likely_response.pronunciation: lah NOHS-trah PEET-tsah kon ee ven-TREE-choh-lah eh OT-tee-mah, lah kon-SEE-lyoh
likely_response.translation_en: Our pizza with ventricola ham is excellent, I recommend it.

responses.english: Our pizza with ventricola ham is excellent, I recommend it.
responses.phonetic: lah NOHS-trah PEET-sah kohn ee vehn-tree-KOH-lah eh OHT-tee-mah, lah kohn-see-LYOH
responses.local: La nostra pizza con i ventricola è ottima, la consiglio.

responses.english: Today we have a white pizza with mushrooms and truffle, seasonal.
responses.phonetic: OH-jee ahb-BYAH-moh OO-nah PEET-sah BYAHN-kah kohn FOON-jee eh trah-TROO-foh, stah-joh-NAH-leh
responses.local: Oggi abbiamo una pizza bianca con funghi e tartufo, stagionale.

phrases.text_local: È possibile avere la pizza da portare via?
phrases.pronunciation: eh poh-SEE-bee-leh ah-VEH-reh lah PEET-sah dah pohr-TAH-reh VEE-ah
phrases.cultural_note: Almost all pizzerias offer takeaway ('da portare via'). In Naples especially, taking pizza away is very common — there are famous pizzerias where everyone takes their pizza to go and eats it on the street.

likely_response.local: Certo! Vuole anche qualcosa da bere?
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh! vwoh-LEH AN-kheh KWAL-koh zah BEH-reh?
likely_response.translation_en: Of course! Would you also like something to drink?

responses.english: Of course! Would you also like something to drink?
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, WOH-leh AHN-keh kwahl-KOH-zah dah BEH-reh
responses.local: Certo! Vuole anche qualcosa da bere?

responses.english: The pizza will be ready in fifteen minutes.
responses.phonetic: lah PEET-sah EH PRAHN-tah trah kween-DEE-chee mee-NOO-tee
responses.local: La pizza è pronta tra quindici minuti.

phrases.text_local: È possibile avere il bordo più croccante?
phrases.pronunciation: eh poh-SEE-bee-leh ah-VEH-reh eel BOHR-doh ploo krohk-KAHN-teh
phrases.cultural_note: Neapolitan pizza has a soft, charred crust ('cornicione') — not the thin crispy base of Roman pizza. If you want extra crispy, ask for 'cotto più' (cooked more). In Naples, the crust is intentionally soft and airy.

likely_response.local: La nostra pizza è cotta nel forno a legna, è già croccante.
likely_response.pronunciation: lah NOHS-trah PEET-tsah eh KOT-tah nel FOR-noh ah LEH-nyah, eh JAH krok-CHAN-teh
likely_response.translation_en: Our pizza is baked in a wood-fired oven, it's already crispy.

responses.english: Our pizza is baked in a wood-fired oven, it's already crispy.
responses.phonetic: lah NOHS-trah PEET-sah eh KOHT-tah nehl FOHR-noh ah LEH-nyah, eh yah krohk-KAHN-teh
responses.local: La nostra pizza è cotta nel forno a legna, è già croccante.

responses.english: Of course, I'll give it one more minute in the oven.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, lyee doh oon mee-NOO-toh plee noohl FOHR-noh
responses.local: Certo, gli do un minuto in più nel forno.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a table for two
phrases.text_local: Ha un tavolo libero per due?
phrases.pronunciation: ah oon TAH-voh-loh LEE-beh-roh pehr DOO-eh
phrases.cultural_note: Always greet with 'Buonasera' (good afternoon/evening) or 'Buongiorno' (good morning) before making requests. Lunch is 12:30-2:30pm, dinner starts 7:30pm+ — arriving earlier means a nearly empty restaurant. Reservations are expected at popular places.

likely_response.local: Sì, si accomodi pure. Ha prenotato?
likely_response.pronunciation: see, see ahk-KOH-moh-dee POO-reh. Ah preh-noh-TAH-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, please take a seat. Do you have a reservation?

responses.english: Yes, please take a seat. Do you have a reservation?
responses.phonetic: see, see ahk-koh-MOH-dee POO-reh. ah preh-noh-TAH-toh
responses.local: Sì, si accomodi pure. Ha prenotato?

responses.english: I'm sorry, it's all occupied. Can I ask how long you can wait?
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, eh TOOT-toh ok-koo-PAH-toh. POH-soh KYEH-deh-reh KWAHN-toh TEHM-poh pwoh ahs-peh-TAH-reh
responses.local: Mi dispiace, è tutto occupato. Posso chiedere quanto tempo può aspettare?

responses.english: For how many people?
responses.phonetic: pehr KWAHN-teh pehr-SOH-neh
responses.local: Per quante persone?

phrases.cultural_note: 'La carta' or 'il menù' both work for menu. In Italy, service charge ('servizio') is often included — check the bottom of the menu. If it says 'servizio non incluso', tipping 5-10% is normal.

likely_response.local: Ecco a lei, buon appetito!
likely_response.pronunciation: EK-koh ah LEH-ee, bwohn ahp-peh-TEE-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Here you are, enjoy your meal!

responses.english: Here you are, enjoy your meal!
responses.phonetic: EH-koh ah lay-EE, bwohn ahp-peh-TEE-toh
responses.local: Ecco a lei, buon appetito!

responses.english: We also have a today's set menu at a fixed price.
responses.phonetic: ahb-BYAH-moh AHN-keh oon meh-NOO dehl JHOHR-noh ah PRET-tsoh FEES-soh
responses.local: Abbiamo anche un menù del giorno a prezzo fisso.

phrases.text_local: Qual è il piatto del giorno?
phrases.cultural_note: The 'piatto del giorno' is often the freshest option and represents good value. Italians take pride in seasonal cooking — what's available changes with the season.

likely_response.local: Oggi abbiamo cacio e pepe fatto in casa.
likely_response.pronunciation: OH-jee ahb-BYAH-moh KAH-choh eh PEH-peh FAH-toh een KAH-zah
likely_response.translation_en: Today we have house-made cacio e pepe.

responses.english: Today we have house-made cacio e pepe.
responses.phonetic: OH-jee ahb-BYAH-moh KAH-choh eh PEH-peh FAHT-toh een KAH-zah
responses.local: Oggi abbiamo cacio e pepe fatto in casa.

responses.english: The chef recommends the seafood risotto.
responses.phonetic: eel SHEF kohn-SEE-lyah eel ree-ZOHT-toh eye froo-TOH-tee dee mah-REH
responses.local: Ilchef consiglia il risotto ai frutti di mare.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about dish ingredients for allergies
phrases.text_local: Sono allergico a..., ho bisogno di sapere cosa contengono i piatti.
phrases.pronunciation: SOH-noh ah-LEHR-jee-koh ah..., oh bee-ZOHG-nyoh dee sah-peh-REH KOH-zah kohn-TEHN-goh ee PYAH-tee
phrases.cultural_note: Common allergens: glutine (gluten), latticini (dairy), uova (eggs), frutta a guscio (nuts), crostacei (shellfish),.soya. Italian kitchens take allergies seriously but always confirm ingredients yourself.

likely_response.local: Certo, vado a chiedere in cucina.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, VAH-doh ah kee-EH-deh-reh een KOO-chee-nah
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll ask the kitchen.

responses.english: Of course, I'll ask the kitchen.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, VAH-doh ah KYEH-deh-reh een koo-CHEE-nah
responses.local: Certo, vado a chiedere in cucina.

responses.english: This dish contains..., I recommend you avoid it.
responses.phonetic: KWEHS-toh PYAH-toh kohn-TYEHN-eh..., leh kohn-see-LYOH dee eh-vee-TAH-reh
responses.local: Questo piatto contiene..., le consiglio di evitare.

responses.english: No problem, we'll prepare everything without.
responses.phonetic: nehs-SOON proh-BLEH-mah, preh-pree-AH-moh TOOT-toh SEHN-tsah
responses.local: Nessun problema, prepariamo tutto senza.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for still or sparkling water
phrases.cultural_note: This is a quintessentially Italian question — you'll be asked at EVERY restaurant. San Pellegrino is the classic sparkling brand. Ordering a bottle of water at dinner is completely normal — tap water is drinkable but Italians rarely drink it at restaurants.

likely_response.local: Naturale o frizzante?
likely_response.pronunciation: nah-too-RAH-leh oh freetz-ZAHN-teh
likely_response.translation_en: Still or sparkling?

responses.english: Still or sparkling?
responses.phonetic: nah-too-RAH-leh oh freet-TSAHN-teh
responses.local: Naturale o frizzante?

responses.english: A bottle or a pitcher?
responses.phonetic: OO-nah boht-TEE-lyah oh OO-nah kah-RAHF-fah
responses.local: Una bottiglia o una caraffa?

phrases.cultural_note: The server will NEVER bring the check unless you ask — this is normal, not rude. They're giving you space to enjoy your meal. Tipping is not obligatory but rounding up or leaving 5-10% is appreciated for good service.

likely_response.local: Ecco a lei, grazie. Buon rientro!
likely_response.pronunciation: EK-koh ah LEH-ee, GRAH-tsyeh. Bwohn ree-EN-troh
likely_response.translation_en: Here you are, thank you. Safe return!

responses.english: Here you are, thank you. Safe return!
responses.phonetic: EH-koh ah lay-EE, GRAH-tsyeh, bwohn ry-EHN-trohs
responses.local: Ecco a lei, grazie. Buon rientro!

responses.english: Are you paying together or separately?
responses.phonetic: PAH-gah ee-SYEH-meh oh seh-pah-rah-TAHM-mehn-teh
responses.local: Paga insieme o separatamente?

responses.english: Is service included?
responses.phonetic: eh een-KLOO-zoh eel sehr-VEE-tsyoh
responses.local: È incluso il servizio?

phrases.cultural_note: Italy is increasingly vegetarian-friendly in cities, but rural areas still have limited options. 'Vegano' for vegan. Many traditional pasta dishes (cacio e pepe, aglio e olio) are naturally vegetarian.

likely_response.local: Certo! Abbiamo vari piatti vegetariani, Le preparo una lista.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh! Ahb-BYAH-moh VAH-ree pee-AH-tee veh-jeh-TAH-ree-AH-nee, leh preh-PAH-roh OO-nah LEE-stah
likely_response.translation_en: Of course! We have several vegetarian dishes, I'll prepare a list for you.

responses.english: Of course! We have several vegetarian dishes, I'll prepare a list for you.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, ahb-BYAH-moh VAH-ree PYAH-tee veh-jeh-tah-RYAH-nee, leh preh-PRAH-oh OO-nah LEE-stah
responses.local: Certo! Abbiamo vari piatti vegetariani, Le preparo una lista.

responses.english: Do you eat fish? We have fresh fish.
responses.phonetic: mahn-JEE-ah PEHS-keh, ahb-BYAH-moh dehl pehs-keh FREHS-koh
responses.local: Mangia pesce? Abbiamo del pesce fresco.

responses.english: Pasta alla norma is vegetarian and delicious.
responses.phonetic: lah PAHS-tah AHL-lah NOHR-mah eh veh-jeh-tah-RYAH-nah eh deh-lee-TSYOH-zah
responses.local: La pasta alla norma è vegetariana e deliziosa.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what the cover charge is
phrases.cultural_note: 'Coperto' (cover charge, €1-4 per person) is a standard charge in Italian restaurants — it covers the bread and table service. It is NOT a tip. Always ask if it's included before protesting. It's listed separately on the menu.

likely_response.local: Il coperto è di due euro a persona, include il pane.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel koh-PEHR-toh eh dee DWEH OO-roh ah per-SOH-nah, een-KLOO-deh eel PAH-neh
likely_response.translation_en: The cover charge is two euros per person, bread is included.

responses.english: The cover charge is two euros per person, bread is included.
responses.phonetic: eel koh-PEHR-toh EH dee DOO-eh EH-oo-roh ah pehr-SOH-nah, een-KLOO-deh lah PAH-neh
responses.local: Il coperto è di due euro a persona, include il pane.

responses.english: Yes, it's included in the cover charge.
responses.phonetic: see, eh een-KLOO-zoh nehl koh-PEHR-toh
responses.local: Sì, è incluso nel coperto.

phrases.intent_en: Say you're still looking at the menu
phrases.text_local: Stiamo ancora guardando il menù, grazie.
phrases.pronunciation: STYAH-moh ahn-KOH-rah gwahr-DAHN-doh eel meh-NOO, GRAH-tsyeh
phrases.cultural_note: Italian servers are attentive but rarely pushy. This polite phrase buys you time without causing offence.

likely_response.local: Certo, prendetevi pure il tempo che volete.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, prehn-DEH-teh-vee POO-reh eel TEHM-poh keh voh-LEH-teh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, take all the time you need.

responses.english: Of course, take all the time you need.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, prehn-DEH-teh-vee POO-reh eel TEHM-poh keh voh-LEH-teh
responses.local: Certo, prendetevi pure il tempo che volete.

responses.english: No problem, I'm right here.
responses.phonetic: nehs-SOON proh-BLEH-mah, SOH-noh KWEE
responses.local: Nessun problema, sono qui.

phrases.cultural_note: Make eye contact or raise a hand slightly. 'Scusi!' (excuse me) is perfectly acceptable to call a waiter in Italy.

likely_response.local: Prego, cosa prende?
likely_response.pronunciation: PREH-goh, KOH-zah PREHN-deh
likely_response.translation_en: Please go ahead, what will you have?

responses.english: Please go ahead, what will you have?
responses.phonetic: PREH-goh, KOH-zah PREHN-deh
responses.local: Prego, cosa prende?

responses.english: I'll be right there!
responses.phonetic: ahr-REE-voh SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Arrivo subito!

phrases.cultural_note: Italian waiters are genuinely knowledgeable and proud of their cuisine. This question often leads to the freshest seasonal dish — trust their recommendation.

likely_response.local: Le consiglio il risotto del giorno, è fatto con ingredienti freschi di stagione.
likely_response.pronunciation: leh kohn-SEE-lyoh eel ree-ZOHT-toh dehl JHOHR-noh, eh FAHT-toh kohn een-greh-DYEHN-tee FREHS-kee dee stah-JYOH-neh
likely_response.translation_en: I recommend today's risotto, it's made with fresh seasonal ingredients.

responses.english: I recommend the risotto of the day, made with fresh ingredients.
responses.phonetic: leh kohn-SEE-lyoh eel ree-ZOHT-toh dehl JHOHR-noh, eh FAHT-toh kohn een-greh-DYEHN-tee FREHS-kee
responses.local: Le consiglio il risotto del giorno, è fatto con ingredienti freschi.

responses.english: The house pasta is our signature dish.
responses.phonetic: lah PAHS-tah DEHL-lah KAH-zah eh eel NOHS-troh PYAH-toh FOR-teh
responses.local: La pasta della casa è il nostro piatto forte.

phrases.cultural_note: Italian menus often use regional or dialect names. Don't hesitate to ask — waiters are happy to explain the dish and its origins.

likely_response.local: È una specialità regionale a base di...
likely_response.pronunciation: eh OO-nah speh-chah-lee-TAH reh-jyoh-NAH-leh ah BAH-zeh dee
likely_response.translation_en: It's a regional speciality made with...

responses.english: It's a regional speciality made with meat and vegetables.
responses.phonetic: eh OO-nah speh-chah-lee-TAH reh-jyoh-NAH-leh ah BAH-zeh dee KAHR-neh eh vehr-DOO-reh
responses.local: È una specialità regionale a base di carne e verdure.

responses.english: It's a dish from local tradition, very flavourful.
responses.phonetic: eh oon PYAH-toh DEHL-lah trah-dee-TSYOH-neh loh-KAH-leh, MOHL-toh sah-poh-REE-toh
responses.local: È un piatto della tradizione locale, molto saporito.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a dish is spicy
phrases.cultural_note: Traditional Italian cuisine is rarely hot-spicy. Exceptions: arrabbiata sauce, 'nduja (Calabrian spicy salami), dishes with peperoncino in the south. 'Piccante' also means 'strong-flavoured' for cheese.

likely_response.local: No, è molto delicato.
likely_response.pronunciation: noh, eh MOHL-toh deh-lee-KAH-toh
likely_response.translation_en: No, it's very mild.

responses.english: No, it's very mild.
responses.phonetic: noh, eh MOHL-toh deh-lee-KAH-toh
responses.local: No, è molto delicato.

responses.english: Yes, it has a little chilli, but not too much.
responses.phonetic: see, ah oon poh dee peh-peh-rohn-CHEE-noh, mah nohn TROHP-poh
responses.local: Sì, ha un po' di peperoncino, ma non troppo.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a dish without an ingredient
phrases.cultural_note: Swap [ingrediente] for: cipolla (onion/chee-POHL-lah), aglio (garlic/AH-lyoh), formaggio (cheese/for-MAH-joh), panna (cream/PAHN-nah), sale (salt/SAH-leh). Italian kitchens are generally accommodating.

likely_response.local: Nessun problema, glielo prepariamo così.
likely_response.pronunciation: nehs-SOON proh-BLEH-mah, LYEH-loh preh-pah-RYAH-moh koh-ZEE
likely_response.translation_en: No problem, we'll prepare it that way for you.

responses.english: No problem, we'll prepare it that way for you.
responses.phonetic: nehs-SOON proh-BLEH-mah, LYEH-loh preh-pah-RYAH-moh koh-ZEE
responses.local: Nessun problema, glielo prepariamo così.

responses.english: I need to check with the kitchen.
responses.phonetic: DEH-voh veh-ree-fee-KAH-reh kohn lah koo-CHEE-nah
responses.local: Devo verificare con la cucina.

phrases.cultural_note: Ask before the bill arrives to make it easier. Most restaurants will accommodate separate checks, especially in tourist areas.

likely_response.local: Certo, quante persone?
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, KWAHN-teh pehr-SOH-neh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, how many people?

responses.english: Of course, how many people?
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, KWAHN-teh pehr-SOH-neh
responses.local: Certo, quante persone?

responses.english: I prefer to do one bill if possible.
responses.phonetic: preh-feh-REES-koh FAH-reh oon KOHN-toh OO-nee-koh, seh pohs-SEE-bee-leh
responses.local: Preferisco fare un conto unico, se possibile.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to split the bill evenly
phrases.cultural_note: You can also say 'Facciamo alla romana' — the Italian idiom for splitting the bill equally among everyone at the table.

likely_response.local: D'accordo, fate alla romana allora!
likely_response.pronunciation: dah-KOR-doh, FAH-teh AHL-lah roh-MAH-nah ahl-LOH-rah
likely_response.translation_en: OK, going Dutch then!

responses.english: OK, going Dutch then!
responses.phonetic: dah-KOR-doh, FAH-teh AHL-lah roh-MAH-nah ahl-LOH-rah
responses.local: D'accordo, fate alla romana allora!

responses.english: That's thirty euros each.
responses.phonetic: SOH-noh TREHN-tah EH-oo-roh ah TEH-stah
responses.local: Sono trenta euro a testa.

phrases.text_local: Tutto insieme su un conto solo.
phrases.pronunciation: TOOT-toh een-SYEH-meh soo oon KOHN-toh SOH-loh
phrases.cultural_note: Say this clearly before anyone else tries to pay — it avoids the scramble for the bill that's common in Italian restaurants.

likely_response.local: Benissimo, Le porto subito il conto.
likely_response.pronunciation: beh-NEES-see-moh, leh POR-toh SOO-bee-toh eel KOHN-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Excellent, I'll bring the bill right away.

responses.english: Excellent, I'll bring the bill right away.
responses.phonetic: beh-NEES-see-moh, leh POR-toh SOO-bee-toh eel KOHN-toh
responses.local: Benissimo, Le porto subito il conto.

responses.english: All together, understood.
responses.phonetic: TOOT-toh een-SYEH-meh, dah-KOR-doh
responses.local: Tutto insieme, d'accordo.

phrases.cultural_note: Al fresco dining is quintessential Italian. In summer, outdoor tables fill quickly — arrive early or book ahead if this matters to you.

likely_response.local: Sì, abbiamo ancora posto in terrazza.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, ahb-BYAH-moh ahn-KOH-rah POHS-toh een teh-RAHT-tsah
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, we still have space on the terrace.

responses.english: Yes, we still have space on the terrace.
responses.phonetic: see, ahb-BYAH-moh ahn-KOH-rah POHS-toh een teh-RAHT-tsah
responses.local: Sì, abbiamo ancora posto in terrazza.

responses.english: I'm sorry, the outdoor tables are all taken.
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, ee TAH-voh-lee FWOH-ree SOH-noh TOOT-tee ohk-koo-PAH-tee
responses.local: Mi dispiace, i tavoli fuori sono tutti occupati.

phrases.cultural_note: Indoor seating is often quieter and cooler in summer. In winter, many Italian outdoor terraces are heated — specify clearly if you want to be inside.

likely_response.local: Certamente, la seguo.
likely_response.pronunciation: chehr-tah-MEHN-teh, lah SEH-gwoh
likely_response.translation_en: Certainly, follow me.

responses.english: Certainly, follow me.
responses.phonetic: chehr-tah-MEHN-teh, lah SEH-gwoh
responses.local: Certamente, la seguo.

responses.english: We have a nice table by the window.
responses.phonetic: ahb-BYAH-moh oon bel TAH-voh-loh vee-CHEE-noh AHL-lah fee-NEHS-trah
responses.local: Abbiamo un bel tavolo vicino alla finestra.

phrases.intent_en: Say you have no reservation and ask about space
phrases.text_local: Non abbiamo la prenotazione — c'è posto?
phrases.pronunciation: nohn ahb-BYAH-moh lah preh-noh-tah-TSYOH-neh — cheh POHS-toh
phrases.cultural_note: Without a reservation, best chances are arriving right at opening (12:30 for lunch, 7:30 for dinner) or trying on a weekday. Popular spots in tourist areas fill fast.

likely_response.local: Vediamo… sì, c'è un tavolo libero.
likely_response.pronunciation: veh-DYAH-moh... see, cheh oon TAH-voh-loh LEE-beh-roh
likely_response.translation_en: Let's see… yes, there's a free table.

responses.english: Let's see… yes, there's a free table.
responses.phonetic: veh-DYAH-moh... see, cheh oon TAH-voh-loh LEE-beh-roh
responses.local: Vediamo… sì, c'è un tavolo libero.

responses.english: I'm sorry, we're fully booked tonight.
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, SYAH-moh ahl kohm-PLEH-toh stah-SEH-rah
responses.local: Mi dispiace, siamo al completo stasera.

phrases.cultural_note: Bread is almost always provided at Italian restaurants, sometimes for a small coperto charge. Refills are usually free — don't hesitate to ask.

likely_response.local: Certo, glielo porto subito.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, LYEH-loh POR-toh SOO-bee-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll bring it right away.

responses.english: Of course, I'll bring it right away.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, LYEH-loh POR-toh SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Certo, glielo porto subito.

responses.english: Here's some fresh bread.
responses.phonetic: EH-koh dehl PAH-neh FREHS-koh
responses.local: Ecco del pane fresco.

phrases.intent_en: Say this isn't what you ordered
phrases.text_local: Questo non è quello che ho ordinato.
phrases.pronunciation: KWEHS-toh nohn eh KWEL-loh keh oh ohr-dee-NAH-toh
phrases.cultural_note: Say this calmly and point to the correct dish on the menu. Italians take food seriously — mistakes will be corrected quickly and apologetically.

likely_response.local: Mi scusi tanto, provvedo subito.
likely_response.pronunciation: mee SKOO-zee TAHN-toh, proh-VEH-doh SOO-bee-toh
likely_response.translation_en: I'm so sorry, I'll sort it right away.

responses.english: I'm so sorry, I'll sort it right away.
responses.phonetic: mee SKOO-zee TAHN-toh, proh-VEH-doh SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Mi scusi tanto, provvedo subito.

responses.english: Did you order the...? I'll check immediately.
responses.phonetic: ah ohr-dee-NAH-toh eel...? veh-ree-FEE-koh SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Ha ordinato il...? Verifico subito.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where an item is located
phrases.cultural_note: Italian supermarkets (Coop, Esselunga, Conad, PAM) are well-stocked. Larger ones have a bakery, butcher, and cheese counter ('bancone') where you can order freshly cut items. Smaller 'alimentari' shops specialize in quality local products.

likely_response.local: È nel corridoio tre, a destra dopo i latticini.
likely_response.pronunciation: eh nel kohr-ree-DOH-yoh TREH, ah DEHS-trah DOH-poh ee laht-tee-CHEE-nee
likely_response.translation_en: It's in aisle three, on the right after the dairy.

responses.english: It's in aisle three, on the right after the dairy.
responses.phonetic: eh nehl kohr-ree-DYOH treh, ah DEH-strah DOH-pah ee laht-tee-CHEE-nee
responses.local: È nel corridoio tre, a destra dopo i latticini.

responses.english: We're out of it, restocking tomorrow morning.
responses.phonetic: lahb-BYAH-moh fee-NEE-toh, ree-fohrnee-MEHN-toh doh-MAH-nee maht-TEE-nah
responses.local: L'abbiamo finito, rifornimento domani mattina.

phrases.text_local: Vorrei prosciutto e formaggio, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: vohr-RAY proh-SHOOT-toh eh fohr-MAH-joh, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Italian supermarkets have staffed deli counters ('bancone') for cold cuts and cheese. You're expected to queue and ask — don't just take from the pre-packaged section. 'Prosciutto cotto' (cooked ham) vs 'prosciutto crudo' (raw/dry-cured ham). Specify how much you want.

likely_response.local: Quanto ne vuole? Due etti, tre etti?
likely_response.pronunciation: KWAHN-toh neh VWOH-leh? DOO-eh EHT-tee, TREH EHT-tee?
likely_response.translation_en: How much do you want? Two hundred grams, three hundred grams?

responses.english: How much do you want? Two hundred grams, three hundred grams?
responses.phonetic: KWAHN-toh neh WOH-leh, DOO-eh EHT-tee, treh EHT-tee
responses.local: Quanto ne vuole? Due etti, tre etti?

responses.english: Freshly sliced or packaged? The fresh one is from today.
responses.phonetic: TAH-lyoh FREHS-koh oh kohn-feh-tsyah-NAH-toh, eel FREHS-koh eh dee OH-jee
responses.local: Taglio fresco o confezionato? Il fresco è di oggi.

phrases.cultural_note: In Italy, you often pay for shopping bags (€0.05-0.10 for plastic, €0.50-1 for reusable). Many Italians bring their own reusable bags ('borsa riutilizzabile'). Bags are usually found near the entrance or at the checkout.

likely_response.local: I sacchetti sono vicino alle casse, sul scaffale a sinistra.
likely_response.pronunciation: ee sahk-KEHT-tee soh-noh vee-CHEE-noh AHL-leh KAHS-seh, sool skahf-FAH-leh ah see-NEE-strah
likely_response.translation_en: Bags are near the checkouts, on the shelf on the left.

responses.english: Bags are near the checkouts, on the shelf on the left.
responses.phonetic: ee sahk-KEHT-tee SOH-noh vee-CHEE-noh AHL-leh KAH-seh, sool sfah-fah-LEH ah see-NEE-strah
responses.local: I sacchetti sono vicino alle casse, sul scaffale a sinistra.

responses.english: Paper bags are free, plastic ones cost ten cents.
responses.phonetic: ee sahk-KEHT-tee dee KAHR-tah SOH-noh grah-twoo-EE-tee, KWEL-lee dee PLAHS-tah-kah koh-STAH-noh DYEH-chee chehn-TEH-zee-mee
responses.local: I sacchetti di carta sono gratuiti, quelli di plastica costano dieci centesimi.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a receipt for a refund
phrases.text_local: Mi può dare lo scontrino? Vorrei un rimborso.
phrases.pronunciation: mee pwoh dah-REH lo skohn-TREE-noh? vohr-RAY oon reem-BOHR-soh
phrases.cultural_note: Italian supermarkets require a receipt ('scontrino') for all returns. Keep your receipt! Without it, returns are generally not possible. Some stores have 'cassa' (checkout) and 'rientro' (returns) at different counters.

likely_response.local: Certo, ha lo scontrino? Senza scontrino non posso effettuare il rimborso.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, ah loh SKOHN-tree-noh? SEHN-tsah skohn-TREE-noh nohn POHS-soh ehf-fet-TWAH-reh eel reem-BOHR-soh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, do you have the receipt? Without a receipt I can't process the return.

responses.english: Of course, do you have the receipt? Without a receipt I can't process the return.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, ah lo skohn-TREE-noh, SEHN-tsah skohn-TREE-noh nohn POH-soh eh-feht-too-AH-reh eel reem-BOHR-soh
responses.local: Certo, ha lo scontrino? Senza scontrino non posso effettuare il rimborso.

responses.english: Go to the returns desk, I'll change it for you with no problem.
responses.phonetic: VAH-dah ahl BAHN-koh ree-POHR-tee, LYEH-loh KAHM-byoh sehn-DALT-troh
responses.local: Vada al banco riporti, glielo cambio senz'altro.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if they accept credit cards
phrases.cultural_note: Most Italian supermarkets accept cards, but some smaller ones (especially in the south or small towns) are cash-only. Look for the card logos on the door. There's usually a minimum purchase (€5-10) for card payments in smaller shops.

likely_response.local: Sì, accettiamo Mastercard, Visa e anche PagoBANCOMAT.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, ah-cheht-TYAH-moh mahs-tehr-KAHRD, VEE-zah eh AHN-keh PAH-goh BAHN-koh-MAHT
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, we accept Mastercard, Visa, and also PagoBANCOMAT.

responses.english: Yes, we accept Mastercard, Visa, and also PagoBANCOMAT.
responses.phonetic: see, ah-cheht-TYAH-moh mah-ster-KAHRd, VEE-zah eh AHN-keh PAH-goh bahn-koh-MAHT
responses.local: Sì, accettiamo Mastercard, Visa e anche PagoBANCOMAT.

responses.english: No, I'm sorry, we only deal with cash.
responses.phonetic: noh, mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, lah-voh-RAH-yoh-moh SOH-loh kohn-TAHN-tee
responses.local: No, mi dispiace, lavoriamo solo con contanti.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the wine/food section is
phrases.cultural_note: Italian supermarkets typically have a dedicated wine section ('sezione vini') with regional selections. 'Alimentari' refers to the food/grocery section. Many Italians buy wine at the supermarket — the quality-to-price ratio is often excellent.

likely_response.local: La sezione vini è in fondo a sinistra, corridoio dodici.
likely_response.pronunciation: lah seh-TSYOH-neh VEE-nee eh een FOHN-doh ah see-NEE-strah, kohr-ree-DOH-yoh DOH-dee-chee
likely_response.translation_en: The wine section is in the back on the left, aisle twelve.

responses.english: The wine section is in the back on the left, aisle twelve.
responses.phonetic: lah seh-TSYOH-neh VEE-nee eh een FOHN-doh ah see-NEE-strah, kohr-ree-DYOH doh-DEE-chee
responses.local: La sezione vini è in fondo a sinistra, corridoio dodici.

responses.english: Groceries are on the ground floor, next to the fresh food counter.
responses.phonetic: lyee ah-lee-mehn-TAH-ree SOH-noh ahl PYAH-noh TEHR-rah, ahk-KAHN-toh ahl BAHN-koh FREHS-koh
responses.local: Gli alimentari sono al piano terra,accanto al banco fresco.

phrases.intent_en: Say you don't need a bag
phrases.text_local: No grazie, non ho bisogno del sacchetto.
phrases.pronunciation: NOH GRAH-tsyeh, nohn oh bee-ZOH-nyoh dehl sahk-KEHT-toh
phrases.cultural_note: Plastic bags in Italian supermarkets are charged for by law. Most shoppers bring their own reusable bags ('borse riutilizzabili'). Saying this at the till saves the charge and is environmentally friendly — the Italian word for shopping bag, 'sacchetto', refers to the small carrier bag.

responses.english: That's fine, thank you.
responses.phonetic: vah BEH-neh, GRAH-tsyeh
responses.local: Va bene, grazie.

responses.english: Do you have a bag with you?
responses.phonetic: ah OO-nah BOR-sah kohn SEH
responses.local: Ha una borsa con sé?

phrases.cultural_note: If a shelf label is missing or unclear, a member of staff ('commesso/a') can scan the barcode. At the deli counter, prices are shown per kilogram and the assistant will weigh and price your selection before handing it over.

responses.english: It costs three euros fifty.
responses.phonetic: KOH-stah TREH EH-oo-roh eh cheen-KWAHN-tah
responses.local: Costa tre euro e cinquanta.

responses.english: I'll scan it for you straight away.
responses.phonetic: LYEH-loh skahn-SYOH-noh SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Glielo scansiono subito.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the organic section is
phrases.text_local: Dove si trova la sezione biologica?
phrases.pronunciation: DOH-veh see TROH-vah lah seh-TSYOH-neh byoh-LOH-jee-kah
phrases.cultural_note: Organic ('biologico') products are clearly labelled in Italian supermarkets. Large chains like Coop and Esselunga have dedicated 'bio' sections. The 'euro leaf' EU organic logo is on all certified products.

responses.english: The organic section is at the back on the left.
responses.phonetic: lah seh-TSYOH-neh byoh-LOH-jee-kah eh een FOHN-doh ah see-NEES-trah
responses.local: La sezione biologica è in fondo a sinistra.

responses.english: The organic products are in aisle three.
responses.phonetic: ee proh-DOHT-tee BEE-oh SOH-noh nehl kor-ree-DOH-yoh TREH
responses.local: I prodotti bio sono nel corridoio tre.

phrases.intent_en: Ask fare and if it’s fixed to a place
phrases.text_local: Quanto costa per andare a...? È un prezzo fisso?
phrases.pronunciation: KWAHN-toh KOH-stah pehr ahn-DAH-reh ah... eh oon PRET-tsoh FEES-soh
phrases.cultural_note: From major Italian airports (Fiumicino, Malpensa), city-center taxi fares are often FIXED ('prezzo fisso') — check the sign at the taxi stand. From train stations and city centers, meters are used. Always confirm before starting if you're unsure.

likely_response.local: Dall'aeroporto di Fiumicino al centro è prezzo fisso: cinquantacinque euro.
likely_response.pronunciation: dahl ah-eh-ROH-pohr-toh dee fyoo-mee-CHEE-noh al CHEN-troh eh PREH-tsoh FEEZ-soh: cheen-KWAHN-tah-CHEEN-kweh OO-roh
likely_response.translation_en: From Fiumicino Airport to the center, it's a fixed price: fifty-five euros.

responses.english: From Fiumicino Airport to the center, it's a fixed price: fifty-five euros.
responses.phonetic: dahl-lah-eh-roh-POHR-toh dee fee-oo-mee-CHEE-noh ahl CHEHN-troh eh PRET-tsoh FEES-soh, cheen-KWAHN-tah-cheen-KWAHN-tah EH-oo-roh
responses.local: Dall'aeroporto di Fiumicino al centro è prezzo fisso: cinquantacinque euro.

responses.english: We use the meter. More or less, about thirty-five euros.
responses.phonetic: oo-ZYAH-moh eel tahs-sah-MEH-troh. PYOO oh MEH-noh, CHEE-krah trehn-tah-CHEEN-kwah EH-oo-roh
responses.local: Usiamo il tassametro. Più o meno, circa trentacinque euro.

phrases.intent_en: Ask driver to wait five minutes
phrases.text_local: Mi può aspettare qui cinque minuti?
phrases.pronunciation: mee pwoh ahs-peht-TAH-reh koo-ee CHEEN-kweh mee-NOO-tee
phrases.cultural_note: It's normal in Italy to ask a taxi to wait while you run into a shop or pick something up. Drivers may agree to wait but expect to be compensated for their time. 'Aspetta un momento' = wait a moment.

likely_response.local: Certo, l'aspettò. Mi paga il tempo di attesa.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, lah-speht-TOH. mee PAH-gah eel TEHM-poh dee ah-TEH-sah
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll wait. You'll pay for the waiting time.

responses.english: Of course, I'll wait. You'll pay for the waiting time.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, lahs-PEHT-toh. mee PAH-gah eel TEHM-poh dee ah-TEH-sah
responses.local: Certo, l'aspettò. Mi paga il tempo di attesa.

responses.english: I'm sorry, I have another client after you. I can't wait.
responses.phonetic: mee dee-SPYAH-cheh, oh oon AH-troh KLyeh-ehn-teh DOH-poh. nohn POH-soh ahs-peht-TAH-reh
responses.local: Mi dispiace, ho un altro cliente dopo. Non posso aspettare.

phrases.intent_en: Ask driver to stop briefly here
phrases.text_local: Può fermarsi un momento qui? Devo solo entrare un minuto.
phrases.pronunciation: pwoh fehr-MAHR-see oon moh-MEHN-toh koo-ee, DEH-voh SOH-loh EHN-trare oon mee-NOO-toh
phrases.cultural_note: Telling a taxi to wait ('fermassi') while you quickly run into a shop is normal. Drivers generally agree — just confirm they'll wait before you get out, and don't take too long.

likely_response.local: Certo, l'aspettò. Però faccia presto, ho fretta.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, lah-speht-TOH. PEH-roh FAH-chah PREHS-toh, oh FREHT-tah
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, I'll wait. But please be quick, I'm in a hurry.

responses.english: Of course, I'll wait. But please be quick, I'm in a hurry.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, lahs-PEHT-toh. peh-ROH FAH-chah PREHS-toh, oh FREHT-tah
responses.local: Certo, l'aspettò. Però faccia presto, ho fretta.

responses.english: OK, I'll stop here. Don't take too long.
responses.phonetic: vah BEH-neh, mee FEHR-moh koo-ee. nohn chee MEHT-tah TROHP-poh
responses.local: Va bene, mi fermo qui. Non ci metta troppo.

phrases.text_local: Mi può dare una ricevuta, per favore?
phrases.pronunciation: mee pwoh dah-REH OO-nah ree-cheh-VOO-tah, pehr pah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Always ask for a receipt ('ricevuta') after a taxi ride — it's useful for expense reports and also the best way to claim lost items. Italian taxis are required to provide receipts.

likely_response.local: Certo, eccola. La ricevuta è digitale, le mando il pdf via email.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, EHK-koh-lah. lah ree-CHEH-vah-tah eh dee-jee-TAH-leh, leh MAHN-doh eel pee-dee-EHF VEE-ah ee-mehl
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, here it is. The receipt is digital, I'll send you the PDF by email.

responses.english: Of course, here it is. The receipt is digital, I'll send you the PDF by email.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, EHK-koh-lah. lah ree-cheh-VOO-tah eh dee-jee-TAH-leh, leh MAHN-doh eel peh-deh-EH-feh VEE-ah eh-MEHL
responses.local: Certo, eccola. La ricevuta è digitale, le mando il pdf via email.

responses.english: Receipts are mandatory, here it is.
responses.phonetic: lah ree-cheh-VOO-tah eh oh-blee-JAH-toh-ree-ah, EHK-koh-lah
responses.local: La ricevuta è obbligatoria, eccola.

phrases.cultural_note: Show the address on your phone if pronunciation is uncertain — Italian drivers are used to this. In Rome you can hail taxis in the street or find them at designated 'posteggio taxi' stands. Always take official white or yellow taxis.

responses.english: OK, it will take about twenty minutes.
responses.phonetic: vah BEH-neh, chee VOH-lyoh-noh CHEER-kah VEHN-tee mee-NOO-tee
responses.local: Va bene, ci vogliono circa venti minuti.

responses.english: Do you know the exact address?
responses.phonetic: koh-NOH-sheh leen-dee-REET-tsoh es-AHT-toh
responses.local: Conosce l'indirizzo esatto?

phrases.intent_en: Ask the driver to turn on the meter
phrases.text_local: Può accendere il tassametro, per favore?
phrases.pronunciation: pwoh ah-CHEHN-deh-reh eel tahs-SAH-meh-troh, pehr fah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Licensed Italian taxis must run the meter. The starting fare varies by city — Rome's meter starts higher than Milan's. Fixed fares apply for journeys to/from major airports — these are officially regulated and posted in the taxi.

responses.english: Yes, here it is.
responses.phonetic: SEE, EH-koh-loh
responses.local: Sì, eccolo.

responses.english: For the airport it's a fixed fare.
responses.phonetic: pehr lah-eh-roh-POR-toh eh OO-nah tah-REEF-fah FEES-sah
responses.local: Per l'aeroporto è una tariffa fissa.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if card payment is accepted
phrases.cultural_note: Card payment in Italian taxis has improved but is not universal — cash ('contanti') is still safer. Ask before you get in if possible. Rome airport taxis and most city taxis in Milan now accept cards, but rural areas may not.

responses.english: Yes, contactless too.
responses.phonetic: SEE, AHN-keh kohn-TAHKT-les
responses.local: Sì, anche contactless.

responses.english: I'm sorry, cash only.
responses.phonetic: mee dees-PYAH-cheh, SOH-loh kohn-TAHN-tee
responses.local: Mi dispiace, solo contanti.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to take the fastest route
phrases.text_local: Prenda il percorso più veloce, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: PREHN-dah eel pehr-KOR-soh pyoo veh-LOH-cheh, pehr fah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Italian taxi drivers know their cities well. If you're in a rush, saying 'ho fretta' (I'm in a hurry) alongside this phrase helps convey urgency. Most will use their phone's navigation app.

responses.english: OK, I'll use the navigator.
responses.phonetic: dah-KOR-doh, OO-zoh eel nah-vee-gah-TOH-reh
responses.local: D'accordo, uso il navigatore.

responses.english: There's traffic, but I'll try.
responses.phonetic: cheh TRAHF-fee-koh, mah chee PROH-voh
responses.local: C'è traffico, ma ci provo.

phrases.cultural_note: AC is standard in Italian licensed taxis. In the summer heat (especially in southern Italy), it's a very common and expected request. You can also ask to open the window — 'può aprire il finestrino?'

responses.english: Of course, straight away.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, SOO-bee-toh
responses.local: Certo, subito.

responses.english: It's already on.
responses.phonetic: eh JAH ah-CHEH-zah
responses.local: È già accesa.

phrases.intent_en: Order a ticket to a city
phrases.cultural_note: Italy's main train operators are Trenitalia (state) and Italo (private). They often use separate stations or tracks — in some cities Trenitalia and Italo depart from different areas. Tickets can also be bought at the station kiosk or from the conductor on board (with a surcharge).

likely_response.local: Solo andata o andata e ritorno?
likely_response.pronunciation: SOH-loh ah-NAH-tah oh ah-NAH-tah eh ree-VEHR-noh?
likely_response.translation_en: One way or round trip?

responses.english: One way or round trip?
responses.phonetic: SOH-loh ahn-DAH-tah oh ahn-DAH-tah eh ree-TOHR-noh
responses.local: Solo andata o andata e ritorno?

responses.english: The train departs from platform seven at ten o'clock.
responses.phonetic: eel TREH-noh PAHR-teh dahl bee-NAH-ryoh SEHT-teh, AHL-leh OH-reh DYEH-chee
responses.local: Il treno parte dal binario sette, alle ore dieci.

responses.english: Do you have a Trenitalia or Italo loyalty card?
responses.phonetic: ah lah KAHR-tah feh-dehl-TAH treh-nee-TAH-lyah oh ee-TAH-loh
responses.local: Ha la carta fedeltà Trenitalia o Italo?

phrases.intent_en: Ask which platform for a city train
phrases.text_local: Da quale binario parte il treno per [city]?
phrases.pronunciation: dah KWAH-leh bee-NAH-ryoh PAHR-teh eel TREH-noh pehr
phrases.cultural_note: 'Binario' = platform. Platforms are announced 10-15 minutes before departure. In large stations (Roma Termini, Milano Centrale), there can be 20+ platforms — check the departures board ('partenze'). Boarding starts 5-10 minutes before departure.

likely_response.local: Controlli il tabellone delle partenze, il binario cambia spesso.
likely_response.pronunciation: kohn-TROHL-lee eel tah-behl-LOH-neh DEH-leh pahr-TEN-tseh, eel bee-NAH-ryoh KYAHM-byah SPOH-seh
likely_response.translation_en: Check the departures board, the platform changes often.

responses.english: Check the departures board, the platform changes often.
responses.phonetic: kohn-TROHL-lee eel tah-behl-LOH-neh DEHL-leh pahr-TEHN-tsee, eel bee-NAH-ryoh KAHM-byah SPEHS-soh
responses.local: Controlli il tabellone delle partenze, il binario cambia spesso.

responses.english: The train to Rome Termini departs from platform four, platform four.
responses.phonetic: eel TREH-noh pehr ROH-mah TEHR-mee-nee PAHR-teh dahl bee-NAH-ryoh KWAHT-troh, bee-NAH-ryoh KWAHT-troh
responses.local: Il treno per Roma Termini parte dal binario quattro, binario quattro.

phrases.intent_en: Check if the train is delayed
phrases.cultural_note: Italian trains are generally reliable but delays do happen — especially in summer when heat can affect tracks. 'In ritardo' = delayed, 'in orario' = on time, 'cancellato' = cancelled. If your train is cancelled, you can get a full refund or rebook for free.

likely_response.local: Sì, purtroppo il treno ha venti minuti di ritardo.
likely_response.pronunciation: see, poor-TOHP-poh eel TREH-noh ah VEN-tee MEE-noo-tee dee ree-TAH-doh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, unfortunately the train is twenty minutes delayed.

responses.english: Yes, unfortunately the train is twenty minutes delayed.
responses.phonetic: see, poort-R Hoh-poh eel TREH-noh ah VEHN-tee mee-NOO-tee dee ree-TAHR-doh
responses.local: Sì, purtroppo il treno ha venti minuti di ritardo.

responses.english: No, it's on time. The train will depart on schedule.
responses.phonetic: noh, eh een oh-RAH-ryoh. eel TREH-noh pahr-tee-RAH poont-wah-LEH
responses.local: No, è in orario. Il treno partirà puntuale.

phrases.cultural_note: Major Italian train stations have 'deposito bagagli' (luggage storage). Prices are typically €5-7 per item for up to 5 hours. Some stations charge by the hour. Smaller stations may not have storage — always check in advance.

likely_response.local: Il deposito bagagli è al binario uno, vicino all'uscita principale.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel deh-POH-zee-toh BAH-gah-lee eh al bee-NAH-ryoh OO-noh, vee-CHEE-noh ah-loo-SHEE-tah preen-chee-PAH-leh
likely_response.translation_en: Luggage storage is at platform one, near the main exit.

responses.english: Luggage storage is at platform one, near the main exit.
responses.phonetic: eel deh-POH-zee-toh bah-GAH-lee eh ahl bee-NAH-ryoh OO-noh, vee-CHEE-noh ahl-loo-SHEE-tah preen-chee-PAH-leh
responses.local: Il deposito bagagli è al binario uno, vicino all'uscita principale.

responses.english: Storage costs six euros per suitcase, maximum five hours.
responses.phonetic: eel deh-POH-zee-toh KOH-stah SEH-ee EH-oo-roh pehr vah-LEE-jah, MAHS-see-moh CHEEN-kweh OH-reh
responses.local: Il deposito costa sei euro per valigia, massimo cinque ore.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the ticket office is
phrases.cultural_note: Biglietteria = ticket office. In large stations, queues can be long — consider using the self-service machines which accept cards and cash and have English language options. Tickets bought on the train from the conductor ('sul treno') cost significantly more.

likely_response.local: La biglietteria è al piano terra, a sinistra degli scaloni.
likely_response.pronunciation: lah bee-lyet-teh-REE-ah eh al PYAH-noh TEHR-rah, ah see-NEE-strah deh-lyee skah-LOH-neh
likely_response.translation_en: The ticket office is on the ground floor, to the left of the stairs.

responses.english: The ticket office is on the ground floor, to the left of the stairs.
responses.phonetic: lah bee-lyeh-teh-REE-ah eh ahl PYAH-noh TEHR-rah, ah see-NEE-strah DEHL-lyee skah-LOH-nee
responses.local: La biglietteria è al piano terra, a sinistra degli scaloni.

responses.english: There are self-service machines at platform three, faster.
responses.phonetic: chee SOH-noh leh mahk-kee-NEHT-teh ow-toh-MAH-tee-cheh ahl bee-NAH-ryoh treh, ploo veh-LOH-cheh
responses.local: Ci sono le macchinette automatiche al binario tre, più veloce.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to change a missed train ticket
phrases.text_local: Ho perso il mio treno. Posso cambiare il biglietto?
phrases.pronunciation: oh PEHR-soh eel MEE-oh TREH-noh. POH-soh kahm-BYAH-reh eel bee-LYEHT-toh
phrases.cultural_note: If you miss your train, Trenitalia tickets for 'Cambi gratuiti' (free exchange) can be changed before departure. Italo tickets vary. Explain calmly — Italian rail staff are generally helpful about legitimate missed connections.

likely_response.local: Certo, può cambiare gratuitamente entro le 24 ore. Ecco il prossimo treno disponibile.
likely_response.pronunciation: CHEHR-toh, pwoh kahm-BYAH-reh grah-TWEE-tah-men-teh EHN-troh leh ven-tee-KWAH-troh OH-reh. EHK-koh eel PROH-see-moh TREH-noh dees-poh-NEE-bee-leh
likely_response.translation_en: Of course, you can change for free within 24 hours. Here's the next available train.

responses.english: Of course, you can change for free within 24 hours. Here's the next available train.
responses.phonetic: CHEHR-toh, pwoh kahm-BYAH-reh grah-twee-tah-MEHN-teh EHN-troh leh vehn-tee-KWAHT-troh OH-reh. EH-koh eel PROHS-see-moh TREH-noh dees-poh-NEE-bee-leh
responses.local: Certo, può cambiare gratuitamente entro le 24 ore. Ecco il prossimo treno disponibile.

responses.english: Your ticket is 'E' category, unfortunately it can't be changed. You need to buy a new one.
responses.phonetic: eel SOO-oh bee-LYEHT-toh eh dee kah-teh-GOH-ree-ah EH, poort-R Hoh-poh nohn eh moh-dee-FEE-kah-bee-leh. DEH-veh kohm-PRAHR-neh OO-noh NWOH-voh
responses.local: Il suo biglietto è di categoria 'E', purtroppo non è modificabile. Deve comprarne uno nuovo.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if seat reservation is required
phrases.cultural_note: On Frecciarossa and Frecciargento high-speed trains, seat reservation is compulsory and included in the ticket. On regional trains ('regionali'), seating is free-for-all — just hop on. Intercity trains fall in between — always check at the ticket desk.

responses.english: Yes, seat reservation is compulsory on this train.
responses.phonetic: SEE, lah preh-noh-tah-TSYOH-neh eh ohb-blee-gah-TOH-ryah soo KWES-toh TREH-noh
responses.local: Sì, la prenotazione è obbligatoria su questo treno.

responses.english: No, you can sit wherever you like.
responses.phonetic: NOH, pwoh seh-DEHR-see DOH-veh VWOH-leh
responses.local: No, può sedersi dove vuole.

phrases.text_local: Un biglietto di andata e ritorno per…, per favore.
phrases.pronunciation: oon beel-YEHT-toh dee ahn-DAH-tah eh ree-TOR-noh pehr..., pehr fah-VOH-reh
phrases.cultural_note: Return ('andata e ritorno') tickets on Trenitalia and Italo high-speed services are often cheaper than two singles. Specify the return date and time. Flexible open returns ('aperto') are available but pricier.

responses.english: When would you like to return?
responses.phonetic: pehr KWAHN-doh VWOH-leh tor-NAH-reh
responses.local: Per quando vuole tornare?

responses.english: First or second class?
responses.phonetic: PREE-mah oh seh-KOHN-dah KLAHS-seh
responses.local: Prima o seconda classe?

phrases.intent_en: Ask about your connection time at a city
phrases.text_local: Ho una coincidenza a… Quanto tempo ho?
phrases.pronunciation: oh OO-nah koh-een-chee-DEHN-tsah ah... KWAHN-toh TEHM-poh oh
phrases.cultural_note: Italian train staff can check your connection in real time. If your train is delayed and you miss a reserved connection, go immediately to the 'biglietteria' or speak to a conductor — Trenitalia will usually rebook you on the next available train at no charge.

responses.english: You have twenty minutes for your connection.
responses.phonetic: ah VEHN-tee mee-NOO-tee dee koh-een-chee-DEHN-tsah
responses.local: Ha venti minuti di coincidenza.

responses.english: If the train is delayed, speak to the conductor.
responses.phonetic: seh eel TREH-noh eh een ree-TAR-doh, PAR-lee kohl kah-poh-TREH-noh
responses.local: Se il treno è in ritardo, parli col capotreno.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to see the timetable for the next trains
phrases.text_local: Può mostrarmi gli orari dei prossimi treni?
phrases.pronunciation: pwoh mohs-TRAR-mee lyee oh-RAH-ree day PROHS-see-mee TREH-nee
phrases.cultural_note: Departure boards ('tabellone partenze') are displayed throughout Italian stations. The Trenitalia app gives real-time updates. At major stations like Roma Termini and Milano Centrale, information desks ('informazioni') have staff who can help in English.

responses.english: The next one leaves at 15:10, platform three.
responses.phonetic: eel PROHS-see-moh PAR-teh AHL-leh KWEEN-dee-chee eh DYEH-chee, bee-NAH-ryoh TREH
responses.local: Il prossimo parte alle quindici e dieci, binario tre.

responses.english: Look at the departures board.
responses.phonetic: GWAR-dee eel tah-behl-LOH-neh dehl-leh par-TEHN-tseh
responses.local: Guardi il tabellone delle partenze.

phrases.cultural_note: Italy's top museums: Uffizi (Florence), Vatican Museums (Rome), Borghese Gallery (Rome), Accademia (Venice). Book online — queues at the door can be hours long at peak times.

likely_response.local: Intero o ridotto?
likely_response.pronunciation: een-TEH-roh oh ree-DOHT-toh
likely_response.translation_en: Full price or reduced?

responses.english: Full price or reduced?
responses.phonetic: een-TEH-roh oh ree-DOHT-toh
responses.local: Intero o ridotto?

responses.english: That's fifteen euros.
responses.phonetic: SOH-noh KWEEN-dee-chee EH-oo-roh
responses.local: Sono quindici euro.

phrases.cultural_note: Most Italian museums allow photography without flash. The Vatican Museums prohibit photos in the Sistine Chapel — strictly enforced. Always check room by room.

likely_response.local: Sì, ma senza flash.
likely_response.pronunciation: SEE, mah SEHN-tsah FLAHSH
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, but no flash.

responses.english: Yes, but no flash.
responses.phonetic: SEE, mah SEHN-tsah FLAHSH
responses.local: Sì, ma senza flash.

responses.english: No, photography is forbidden in this room.
responses.phonetic: NOH, een KWEHS-tah SAH-lah eh vyeh-TAH-toh
responses.local: No, in questa sala è vietato.

phrases.cultural_note: Audio guides are standard in major Italian museums. Many now offer free app-based guides — ask at the ticket desk. The Vatican audio guide (€7) is worth every cent.

likely_response.local: Sì, costa sette euro.
likely_response.pronunciation: SEE, KOH-stah SEHT-teh EH-oo-roh
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, it costs seven euros.

responses.english: Yes, it costs seven euros.
responses.phonetic: SEE, KOH-stah SEHT-teh EH-oo-roh
responses.local: Sì, costa sette euro.

responses.english: There's also a free app to download.
responses.phonetic: cheh AHN-keh lahp grah-TWEE-tah dah skah-ree-KAH-reh
responses.local: C'è anche l'app gratuita da scaricare.

phrases.cultural_note: Large bags must be left at the 'guardaroba' (cloakroom) — this is mandatory at most Italian museums. It's free and secure.

likely_response.local: Il guardaroba è all'ingresso, a sinistra.
likely_response.pronunciation: eel gwahr-dah-ROH-bah eh ahl-een-GREHS-soh, ah see-NEES-trah
likely_response.translation_en: The cloakroom is at the entrance, on the left.

responses.english: The cloakroom is at the entrance, on the left.
responses.phonetic: eel gwahr-dah-ROH-bah eh ahl-een-GREHS-soh, ah see-NEES-trah
responses.local: Il guardaroba è all'ingresso, a sinistra.

responses.english: It's free and secure.
responses.phonetic: eh grah-TWEE-toh eh see-KOO-roh
responses.local: È gratuito e sicuro.

phrases.text_local: Quali sono gli orari di apertura?
phrases.pronunciation: KWAH-lee SOH-noh lyee oh-RAH-ree dee ah-pehr-TOO-rah
phrases.cultural_note: Most Italian state museums close on Mondays. The Vatican is closed on Sundays except the last Sunday of the month (free entry). Book ahead for popular sites.

likely_response.local: Siamo aperti dalle nove alle diciassette.
likely_response.pronunciation: SYAH-moh ah-PEHR-tee DAHL-leh NOH-veh AHL-leh dee-chahs-SEHT-teh
likely_response.translation_en: We're open from 9am to 5pm.

responses.english: We're open from 9am to 5pm.
responses.phonetic: SYAH-moh ah-PEHR-tee DAHL-leh NOH-veh AHL-leh dee-chahs-SEHT-teh
responses.local: Siamo aperti dalle nove alle diciassette.

responses.english: We're closed on Mondays.
responses.phonetic: eel loo-neh-DEE SYAH-moh KYOO-zee
responses.local: Il lunedì siamo chiusi.

phrases.cultural_note: Italian state museums offer free entry to EU citizens under 18 and heavily discounted tickets for under-26s. Many offer free entry on the first Sunday of the month ('Domenica al museo').

likely_response.local: Sì, i giovani sotto i 26 anni pagano la metà.
likely_response.pronunciation: SEE, ee JOH-vah-nee SOHT-toh ee VEHN-tee-SEY AHN-nee PAH-gah-noh lah meh-TAH
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, young people under 26 pay half price.

responses.english: Yes, young people under 26 pay half price.
responses.phonetic: SEE, ee JOH-vah-nee SOHT-toh ee vehn-tee-SEY AHN-nee PAH-gah-noh lah meh-TAH
responses.local: Sì, i giovani sotto i 26 anni pagano la metà.

responses.english: The first Sunday of the month is free.
responses.phonetic: lah PREE-mah doh-meh-NEE-kah dehl MEH-zeh eh grah-TWEE-tah
responses.local: La prima domenica del mese è gratuita.

phrases.cultural_note: Guided tours in Italian museums often need advance booking. English-language tours are common in major tourist sites. Private guides can be hired at the entrance of major museums.

likely_response.local: Sì, la prossima visita guidata è alle tre.
likely_response.pronunciation: SEE, lah PROHS-see-mah vee-ZEE-tah gwee-DAH-tah eh AHL-leh TREH
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, the next guided tour is at 3pm.

responses.english: Yes, the next guided tour is at 3pm.
responses.phonetic: SEE, lah PROHS-see-mah vee-ZEE-tah gwee-DAH-tah eh AHL-leh TREH
responses.local: Sì, la prossima visita guidata è alle tre.

responses.english: English tours are at 11am and 4pm.
responses.phonetic: leh vee-ZEE-teh een een-GLEH-zeh SOH-noh AHL-leh OON-dee-chee eh AHL-leh KWAHT-troh
responses.local: Le visite in inglese sono alle undici e alle quattro.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where a specific exhibit is
phrases.cultural_note: Show the name on your phone or brochure if pronunciation is difficult. Free maps ('mappe') are usually available at the ticket desk.

likely_response.local: È al primo piano, ala destra.
likely_response.pronunciation: eh ahl PREE-moh PYAH-noh, AH-lah DEHS-trah
likely_response.translation_en: It's on the first floor, right wing.

responses.english: It's on the first floor, right wing.
responses.phonetic: eh ahl PREE-moh PYAH-noh, AH-lah DEHS-trah
responses.local: È al primo piano, ala destra.

responses.english: Here's the museum map.
responses.phonetic: EH-koh lah MAHP-pah dehl moo-ZEH-oh
responses.local: Ecco la mappa del museo.
